title: 3 Things to Forget
author: Cynthia T. Toney
summary: In Alaska, Wendy thinks she’s left behind the problems resulting from her mistakes in Louisiana, at least for a while. But starting the summer at her friend Sam’s house and volunteering with Alaska Wildlife Conservation bring not only strange surroundings but also strangers into her life. And those strangers have a secret involving a troubled girl who threatens Wendy’s friendship with Sam. As Wendy struggles to understand the Alaskans she meets and gets to know, will she be able to forget what she hopes to, or will her new challenges teach her the importance of remembering the past?

title: 6 Dates to Disaster
author: Cynthia T. Toney
summary: When Wendy’s family faces financial hardship, she must find a way to see Mrs. V and Sam again—but will she lose David forever in the process? For her mom’s birthday, Wendy finds an old jewelry box at a flea market—the perfect gift for someone who loves salvaged junk. But inside the box is a cryptic note that appears to have been written recently. Wendy’s curiosity leads her on a search with boyfriend David at her side, eager to help. Who wrote the note, and did the intended recipient ever see it? But when Wendy’s stepfather loses his job, she needs more personal and urgent help—the financial kind. The family’s plan to visit Alaska on vacation is headed down the sewer like a hard Louisiana rain. How will Wendy ever see Mrs. V or Sam again? An opportunity arrives in the form of tutoring Melissa, one of the Sticks, and Wendy’s money problems appear to be solved. Until the arrangement takes a turn that gets Wendy into trouble like never before. In the final months of ninth grade, she might lose everything she counted on for the future.

title: 9 Curious Kittens in the Bedroom and Bathroom
author: Laura Blum
summary: The 9 Curious Kittens are back, this time playing in a bedroom and bathroom! Little ones LOVE looking for the kittens and little do they know, they are learning prepositions and how to count 1-10! Be sure to scan the QR code on the back of each book to learn the signs for bedroom and bathroom vocabulary and numbers 1-10. This book is written in English to be simple to sign and help children be school ready. (Only the title of this book rhymes when signed.)

title: 9 Curious Kittens in the Kitchen
author: Laura Blum
summary: The kittens are back and this time- they are exploring the KITCHEN! Find all the kittens while learning Kitchen vocabulary, OPPOSITES and how to count 1-10! Be sure to scan the QR code on the back of each book to learn the signs for kitchen vocabulary and numbers 1-10. This book is written in English to be simple to sign and help children be school ready. (Only the title of this book rhymes when signed.)

title: 9 Curious Kittens in the Living Room
author: Laura Blum
summary: Little ones LOVE looking for the kittens in the living room! Little do they know, they are learning prepositions and how to count 1-10! Be sure to scan the QR code on the back of each book to learn the signs for living room vocabulary and numbers 1-10. This book is written in English to be simple to sign and help children be school ready. (Only the title of this book rhymes when signed.)

title: 10 Steps to Girlfriend Status
author: Cynthia T. Toney
summary: Without knowing or caring where I’d wind up, I sneaked out of the house and took off running." Wendy Robichaud is on schedule to have everything she wants in high school: two loyal best friends, a complete and happy family, and a hunky boyfriend she’s had a crush on since eighth grade—until she and Mrs. Villaturo look at old photo albums together. That’s when Mrs. V sees her dead husband and hints at a scandal down in Cajun country. Faster than you can say “crawdad,” Wendy digs into the scandal. She risks losing boyfriend David by befriending Mrs. V’s cute grandson, alienates stepsister Alice by having a boyfriend in the first place, and upsets her friend Gayle without knowing why. Will Wendy be able to prevent Mrs. V from being taken thousands of miles away from her? And will she lose all the friends she’s fought so hard to get?

title: A day at the Zoo
author: Don McNamara
authorConnection: Grandparent of D/HH Child of D/HH Child

title: A day at School
author: Don McNamara
authorConnection: Grandparent of D/HH Child of D/HH Child

title: A day ends at Bedtime
author: Don McNamara
authorConnection: Grandparent of D/HH Child of D/HH Child

title: A day starts with Breakfat
author: Don McNamara
authorConnection: Grandparent of D/HH Child of D/HH Child

title: A day with Colors
author: Don McNamara
authorConnection: Grandparent of D/HH Child of D/HH Child

title: A day with our Family
author: Don McNamara
authorConnection: Grandparent of D/HH Child of D/HH Child

title: A Deaf Child's Journey to Hearing: Meet Ruby's Family
author: Cochlear Implanted Children's Support Group-UK

title: A Drowned Maiden's Hair: A Melodrama
author: Laura Amy Schlitz
summary: Maud Flynn is known at the orphanage for her impertinence. So when the charming Miss Hawthorne chooses her to take home, the girl is pleased but baffled, until it becomes clear that she’s needed to help stage elaborate séances for bereaved patrons. As Maud is drawn deeper into the deception, playing her role as a "secret child," she is torn between her need to please and her growing conscience – until a shocking betrayal shows just how heartless her so-called guardians are. Filled with fascinating details of turn-of-the-century spiritualism and page-turning suspense, this lively novel features a feisty heroine whom readers will not soon forget.

title: A Journey Into the Deaf-World
author: Harlan Lane, Ben Bahan, Robert Hoffmeister

title: A Loss for Words: The Story of Deafness in a Family
author: Lou Ann Walker

title: A New Classmate: All Kinds of Languages
author: Lisa Bullard

title: A Puppy Like Yours
author: Frank Murphy, Carla Murphy
summary: Dogs are more than pets or furry fixtures around the house. Dogs are constant companions who show us what it means to be curious, enjoy the little moments of life, and care for those who need us. Dogs teach us more about life than we teach them. So no matter what your puppy looks like or plays like—this book celebrates all the puppies out there and the ways they change our lives for the better.

title: A Quiet Foghorn: More Notes from a Deaf Gay Life
author: Raymond Luczak

title: A Really Nice Prom Mess
author: Brian Sloan
summary: Cameron doesn't want to go to prom. Not with his boyfriend, Shane, and definitely not with his fake date, Virginia. Sure, it's senior prom, it's the end of high school, and Virginia's drop-dead gorgeous. But none of that matters to Cam, who's never liked any high school dance. Ever. Then an unexpected kiss changes everything, and Cam needs to make a quick exit. After teaming up with a waiter who's been dealing drugs in the bathroom, Cam leaves the prom. But his night is far from over. From a high-speed car chase, to a stop at the after-prom party, to a bar with a wild dance contest...Cam's night finally ends in the most unlikely of romances.

title: A Sign of Affection (10 Volume Series)
author: suu Morishita

title: A Sign to Remember
author: Ron Podmore
summary: Explore the fictional story of Joseph, a 15 year-old Deaf, Sacajawea High School student, growing up in the Puyallup Valley. Not only is he learning to make the most of his hearing loss, but he is also learning American Sign Language. This in addition, he must cope with learning how to drive, and make decisions in a world increasingly fraught with major changes. His day is filled with thoughts of the pretty girl in his Math class named Jessica, and his desires for a date with her. However, life isn't all roses. He soon discovers through a series of incidences that he carries the genes called Usher Syndrome, one that will ultimately render him blind.

title: A Sudden Silence
author: Eve Bunting
summary: Jesse Harmon is tortured by guilt because he survived the hit-and-run accident that killed his brother, Bry. His guilt is compounded when he finds he is attracted to Bry's girlfriend, Chloe. Together Jesse and Chloe try to track down the drunk driver who killed Bry--but discovering the driver's identity leads to other disturbing truths.

title: A Voice Like Yours
author: Frank Murphy, April Groman
summary: Each of us has a unique and powerful voice, whether we speak loudly or softly, sing or sign, or without any words at all. What matters is how we use that voice--because voices are meant to be used. Some of us use it to express ourselves through art. And some use their voices to vote and make big changes. Others use their voice to inspire or lift up others, helping other voices to be heard. Your voice is yours alone--and the world needs a voice like yours.

title: A Whole New World of Sound
author: M.D. MacLeod

title: ABCs of American Sign Language: Medical Edition
author: Chaturika Liyanage

title: Aces Wild: A Heist
author: Amanda DeWitt
summary: Some people join chess club, some people play football. Jack Shannon runs a secret blackjack ring in his private school’s basement. What else is the son of a Las Vegas casino mogul supposed to do? Everything starts falling apart when Jack’s mom is arrested for their family’s ties to organized crime. His sister Beth thinks this is the Shannon family’s chance to finally go straight, but Jack knows that something’s not right. His mom was sold out, and he knows by who. Peter Carlevaro: rival casino owner and jilted lover. Gross. Jack hatches a plan to find out what Carlevaro’s holding over his mom’s head, but he can’t do it alone. He recruits his closest friends—the asexual support group he met through fandom forums. Now all he has to do is infiltrate a high-stakes gambling club and dodge dark family secrets, while hopelessly navigating what it means to be in love while asexual. Easy, right?

title: Ada and the Helpers
author: Travis D Peterson
summary: "Be bold! Be brave! Let you be you... and let's help others, too!" That's Ada's motto.Ada is a dancing, deaf fox with cochlear implants who loves to help others. On her way to dance in a talent show one day, she meets three other creatures who are each facing a physical challenge or disability of their own. She decides to help each of her new friends to see past their challenges and discover their natural, God-given strengths. But can they help her in return? Ada's a bit nervous about the talent show! Will she be able to hear the music clearly? Will everyone laugh at her? Pick up a copy of Ada and the Helpers today and follow Ada and her new friends on an exciting new adventure that inspires friendship, helping others, inclusion, and finding your strengths.

title: Alandra's Lilacs: The Story of a Mother and Her Deaf Daughter
author: Tressa Bowers

title: Alex and the Good Day: A day in the life with cochlear implants
author: Luna Solarian

title: Ali and Aidy Go to the Beach
author: Hameeda Raj

title: All the Way to the Top: How One Girl's Fight for Americans with Disabilities Changed Everything
author: Annette Bay Pimentel

title: All the Ways I Hear You
author: Stephanie Marrufo

title: Alone in the Mainstream: Looking Back on Public School as a Deaf or Hard of Hearing Child
author: Gina A. Oliva

title: Alphabet in English & Sign!
author: Laura Blum
summary: We have partnered with Deaf educators to come up with signs that share the SAME ASL handshape as the English Alphabet. This book is NOT designed to teach the sounds of each letter. Rather this book bridges ASL and English. For example, A= Ape (and the handshape is the same.) B = Blue, C = Cookie, etc. This repetition of handshape will help you learn your ABC’s!

title: Amazing Alana and Her Super Ears: A Hearing Aid Story Book
author: Elizabeth Yeung

title: Anna and Her Power
author: N.D. Loren
summary: "Anna and her power" tells the story of a girl who was born with a problem in her ears that prevented her from hearing clearly. One day, everything changes when she is fitted with hearing aids that will allow her to hear everything for the first time. With her new superpower, Ana discovers the beauty of sounds and learns to appreciate the wonderful in the everyday. A touching story about the strength and bravery of a girl to face challenges and discover her own superpower.

title: Annie Sullivan and the Trials of Helen Keller
author: Joseph Lambert

title: Archie the Sound Super Hero: A Story about Cochlear Implants
author: Elizabeth Yeung

title: Articulate: A Deaf Memoir of Voice
author: Rachel Renee Kolb

title: Assembly Required: Notes From a Deaf Gay Life
author: Raymond Luczak

title: At the Bottom of my Garden
author: Sue Searle

title: Babella's Umbrella
author: Hadley Barrows
summary: Babella is ready to use her red umbrella and her blue hearing aid to listen to the pitter pat of rain. She's just missing one thing -- a raincloud. Join Babella and the friends who help her wait in this soothing story about friendship, patience and listening. The LingSix sounds, [m], [ah], [oo], [ee], [sh] and [s], are part of almost every word we say. Babella uses a hearing aid to hear all sounds, including these LingSix sounds. Like Babella, children who are deaf or hard of hearing may use hearing aids or cochlear implants to hear. Each of the six Ling sounds can be found in Babella's Umbrella so children can practice these LingSix sounds as they develop their hearing and language skills.

title: Baby and Bunny
author: Mimi Brian Vance
summary: Teaching American Sign Language to babies through interactive stories, this series of concept books weaves five essential signs into the story of each book, enabling the adult reader to learn sign language while teaching babies to sign long before they learn to form words. Providing a context for the introduction of each sign, these educational stories focus on communicating essential messages through repetition and reading aloud while encouraging long-term developmental benefits by stimulating early communication skills.

title: Baby's Feelings Book
author: Laura Blum
summary: Little ones often have BIG feelings and learning to recognize and name a feeling is the first step to being able to regulate feelings. Baby’s Feelings Book is simple to sign in American Sign Language- parents can share this book with zero previous ASL classes. Scan the QR code and watch a video by the Deaf Artist CarloJose to learn the signs! (Video is less than a minute long.) All the Feelings shared in this book use the same (modified handshape) and RHYME when SIGNED in ASL!

title: Baby's Imagination
author: Laura Blum
summary: Have you ever wondered what a baby is thinking? Skillful fine artist Benjamin Davis offers a possible insight into a Baby’s Imagination. Ben is a Deaf artist who also has cerebral palsy. We are thrilled he was willing to share his masterpiece paintings spotlighted in this (mostly) wordless book. Baby’s Imagination allows families opportunities to play with ASL Classifiers! Take your storytelling to the next level using classifiers with this simple-to-sign book! Don’t know how? Scan the QR code in this book to learn from a Deaf Mentor!

title: Bear Goes to School
author: Bryony Walker
authorConnection: Godparent of D/HH Child of D/HH Child

title: Becoming a Super Sibling
author: Chloe Zingale
summary: Becoming a Super Sibling brings the reader into the world of a child growing up with a sibling who is wonderfully different. This heartwarming story offers hope and inspiration for families facing a challenging diagnosis—and for those who want to lend their support. Written from the perspective of nine-year-old Chloe, in kid-friendly language that is easy to understand, big sister Chloe explains facial differences, cleft palate, BAHA/Ponto hearing aids, and more. Chloe shares her journey of Becoming a Super Sibling, including the initial days in the NICU, the endless doctor’s appointments, long boring car rides to the children’s hospital, as well as the unexpected joy of meeting new friends in waiting rooms . . . And of course, the ways she helps her sister! Becoming A Super Sibling does more than merely tell a story: It fosters understanding, acceptance, and respect for the differences that make us unique. It presents a narrative of resilience, love, acceptance, and the extraordinary strength of a family in the face of daunting challenges.

title: Begin, End, Begin: A LoveOzYA Anthology
author: Danielle Binks

title: Being Jazmine
author: Cecily Anne Paterson
summary: Jazmine's deaf. And she's getting tired. Tired of having to try hard, tired of fitting in, tired of pretending to be like everyone else.When Jaz goes to deaf camp, a new world opens up to her. But when you leave one world and enter another, what happens to the people you leave behind? Which world will Jaz live in? Can she keep a foot in both? How will she figure out the best way to be Jazmine?

title: Being Seen: One DB Woman's Fight to End Ableism
author: Elsa Sjunneson

title: Between Two Worlds: My Life as a Child of Deaf Adults
author: David Sorenson
summary: In his memoir, David Sorensen explores his identity as a coda, or a child of Deaf adults. He describes his experiences with the roles often placed on codas at a young age, such as interpreter, confidant, and decision-maker. His story reveals a person seeking acceptance and belonging while straddling the Deaf and hearing worlds, and shows how he found reconciliation within himself and with both worlds. Sorensen relays the dynamics of his family life; he had a strained relationship with his father, who was an active leader and role model in the Deaf community and the Mormon Church, yet struggled to bond with his own son. Sorensen rebelled as a youth and left home as a teenager, completely detaching from the Deaf community. After struggling to establish himself as an independent adult, he discovered that he wanted to return to the Deaf world and use his ASL fluency and cultural understanding as a mental health therapist and community advocate. Now he considers himself an ambassador between the Deaf and hearing worlds, as well as between the older and younger generations of Deaf people. Between Two Worlds: My Life as a Child of Deaf Adults shares the unique experiences of a coda and passes on the rich cultural past shared by the American Deaf community.

title: Beyond the Portal
author: David K. Anderson
summary: Christy’s parents forbid her from returning through the portal, but her grandfather is still stranded in the Empty World. When Danny and Brad accidentally take a trip into the portal, Christy finds the excuse she needs to disobey her parents and go after them. Unfortunately for Christy, she's stuck with Cory for a traveling companion. Thus begins another journey through the Empty World, looking for the people they've lost, and for a way back home. Along the way, they'll have to deal with the two intelligent species native to that alien world, and unravel the workings of the ancients' portals. Can Christy get them all safely home, or will her feud with Cory result in all of them being lost for good?

title: Beyond the Sky's Limit
author: Jim Bombicino
summary: A second collection of timely and targeted comic strips highlighting situations and predicaments that will ring true with the Hard of Hearing teens in your life. A must for parents, teachers, and counselors when opening discussion about how to improve communication and use simple strategies for better understanding. Check it out! Available for purchase as ebook or PPT at https//teachertoolstakeout.com

title: Billie BAHA Goes to the Movies
author: Jessica Jordan-Hogan
summary: Join Billie BAHA and the rest of her Super HEARo friends as they take a field trip to the movies. They will encounter an obstacle, learn about their ADA rights, practice their self-advocacy skills and come together to resolve their problems!

title: Bitsy Bat, Team Star
author: Kaz Windness
summary: Bitsy meets a new nonspeaking student who uses an AAC device and sign language at school and learns the importance of being a star teammate in this sequel to Bitsy Bat, School Star. Bitsy Bat is a star student and the only fantastic flier at Crittercrawl Elementary…until a new nonspeaking student named Enzo Owl arrives and outflies her during recess. Bitsy worries her friends won’t think she’s special anymore, so she’s determined to beat Enzo in the upcoming Critter Games and prove she’s the better flier. But when her friend Mo needs help during the race, Bitsy must choose between winning and being a star teammate. And while she’s at it, she just might discover that making a new friend is more special than any trophy.

title: Blossom Beyond Sound: The Beauty of Growing in Our Own Way with Silence
author: Ashlee Rose Holland
summary: Blossom Beyond Sound is a heartfelt and empowering book created especially for Deaf and Hard of Hearing girls. Through inspiring letters, personal stories, helpful tips, and beautiful artwork, this book celebrates every girl’s unique journey with hearing loss. From navigating identity and confidence to embracing silence and self-expression, Blossom Beyond Sound offers encouragement and connection for girls blooming in their own way. Written by a profoundly dDeaf author Ashlee Rose Holland who grew up using the oral approach, this book is a safe space of understanding, honesty, and strength. Whether you wear hearing aids, cochlear implants, or nothing at all—this book is here to remind you: you are enough, just as you are.

title: Blue Lily, Lily Blue
author: Maggie Stiefvater
summary: Blue Sargent has found things. For the first time in her life, she has friends she can trust, a group to which she can belong. The Raven Boys have taken her in as one of their own. Their problems have become hers, and her problems have become theirs.The trick with found things, though, is how easily they can be lost.Friends can betray.Mothers can disappear.Visions can mislead.Certainties can unravel.

title: Boat and Bath
author: Mimi Brian Vance
summary: Teaching American Sign Language to babies through interactive stories, this series of concept books weaves five essential signs into the story of each book, enabling the adult reader to learn sign language while teaching babies to sign long before they learn to form words. Providing a context for the introduction of each sign, these educational stories focus on communicating essential messages through repetition and reading aloud while encouraging long-term developmental benefits by stimulating early communication skills.

title: Bonnie and the Deaf Bake Squad
author: Mickey Carolan

title: Book and Bed
author: Mimi Brian Vance
summary: Teaching American Sign Language to babies through interactive stories, this series of concept books weaves five essential signs into the story of each book, enabling the adult reader to learn sign language while teaching babies to sign long before they learn to form words. Providing a context for the introduction of each sign, these educational stories focus on communicating essential messages through repetition and reading aloud while encouraging long-term developmental benefits by stimulating early communication skills.

title: Book One: Moon
author: Aaron Ehasz, Melanie McGanney Ehasz
summary: WAR IS COMING . . . Four full moons past, humans crossed into the magical lands of Xadia and committed an unspeakable crime: They destroyed the only egg of the Dragon King and Queen. Now a young Moonshadow elf assassin has been sent on her first mission; she will make the humans pay for their heinous act. But before she can complete her task, she and two human princes make an astonishing discovery . . . a discovery that could change everything. And so the three reluctant allies set off in a desperate attempt to stop the coming war. Their journey won't be easy, but the trio soon learns that the most serious threat to their quest can't be fought with magic or physical strength. Can these young heroes overcome the longstanding hatred between humans and elves, and restore peace to their world? Written by Aaron Ehasz (co-creator of The Dragon Prince and head writer of Avatar: The Last Airbender) and Melanie McGanney Ehasz, this first canon novel based on the Netflix original series finally gives fans the full story.

title: Book Two: Sky
author: Aaron Ehasz, Melanie McGanney Ehasz
summary: XADIA IS CALLING... The Dragon Prince has hatched! Now the princes of Katolis, Callum and Ezran, along with Moonshadow elf Rayla, have one goal: deliver the defenseless dragon to his mother in the magical land of Xadia. Things get complicated when the High Mage’s children, Claudia and Soren, track down the questing princes. Should Callum and Ezran trust two humans they’ve known forever, or the elf they’ve just met? In Katolis, High Mage Lord Viren schemes to gain the support of the other human kingdoms, and that of a much more mysterious ally...The tensions of war between Xadia and the Human Kingdoms are ready to explode. As fiery battles erupt and hidden truths come to light, friendships will be tested, plans will be set into motion, and everyone will face their most difficult choices yet.

title: Bounce & Jiggle
author: Sanja Rescek
summary: Touch is a baby's first language... Designed to stimulate the senses, and based on research into the benefits of baby exercise and massage, these books encourage interaction between parent and baby. Devoting time to special activities with your baby plays a crucial part in the bonding process. Singing, accompanied by energetic activities, encourages babies to express their emotions, release tension - and just enjoy being noisy!

title: Breaking the Sound Barriers: 9 Deaf Success Stories
author: Julie Postance

title: Bruised
author: Tanya Boteju
summary: To Daya Wijesinghe, a bruise is a mixture of comfort and control. Since her parents died in an accident she survived, bruises have become a way to keep her pain on the surface of her skin so she doesn’t need to deal with the ache deep in her heart. So when chance and circumstances bring her to a roller derby bout, Daya is hooked. Yes, the rules are confusing and the sport seems to require the kind of teamwork and human interaction Daya generally avoids. But the opportunities to bruise are countless, and Daya realizes that if she’s going to keep her emotional pain at bay, she’ll need all the opportunities she can get. The deeper Daya immerses herself into the world of roller derby, though, the more she realizes it’s not the simple physical pain-fest she was hoping for. Her rough-and-tumble teammates and their fans push her limits in ways she never imagined, bringing Daya to big truths about love, loss, strength, and healing.

title: Burt the Bucksin Gets Hearing Aids
author: E.C. Wall & A.M. Wall
series: The Adventures of Eddie and Charlie
summary: Burt the Buckskin was born with a hearing loss. He’s always loved the ocean, but he’s never truly heard its waves crash or the seagulls' call. That’s about to change—Burt is getting hearing aids for the very first time! Join Burt and his friends, Eddie and Charlie, on a heartwarming journey as he discovers the amazing sounds he’s been missing. From the whisper of the breeze to the mighty roar of the sea, Burt’s world is about to get a whole lot bigger! This book supports and encourages children with hearing loss as they embark on the journey of getting hearing aids. That first trip to the audiologist can feel overwhelming--but like Burt, kids will see that it's also an exciting adventure filled with amazing new sounds to discover!

title: But You Look So Normal: Lost and Found in a Hearing World
author: Claudia Marseille

title: Camden and His Superpowers go to a New School
author: Leigh Porter Cutrone

title: Camden and His Superpower Ears and a Superpower Team
author: Leigh Porter Cutrone

title: Camp Twisted Pine
author: Ciera Burch
summary: Eleven-year-old Naomi loves all things outdoors—birds and beetles, bats and bunnies—in theory. She explores nature in the best possible way: the cold, hard facts in books. So when her parents’ announcement of their impending divorce comes hand in hand with sending Naomi and her younger twin brothers to summer camp while they figure things out, it’s salt in the wound for Naomi and her avoidance of hands-on experience. Camp Twisted Pine could be worse. The counselors are nice, and Naomi likes her cabinmates, especially Jackie, whose blunt personality and frank dislike of the camp draw Naomi in quickly. Jackie is also hard of hearing and uses a hearing aid, and the girls quickly develop a routine of sign language lessons in their free time, which Naomi sees as a welcome break when all the s’mores-making and nature walks get to be a bit much. But the campers aren’t the only ones who roam the grounds of Camp Twisted Pine. When people start to go missing, including Jackie, Naomi has to find a way to save everyone—and herself. Her practical knowledge of the outdoors may still be rudimentary at best, but she has years of studying and the scientific method to fall back on. Can Naomi identify and stop the dangerous predator before it’s too late?

title: Can You Feel It?
author: Pauline Findlay
summary: What happens when a London rock star, falls in love with an ordinary Australian girl? Can Lola feel the music, before it's too late? Lola has a secret. It's not that her mother has kicked her out of home. Or that her new housemates are dead people. It's not hiding her growing feelings for Kain, the punk rocker who's recently arrived in town and turned her world upside down. Lola is going deaf, which for a musician, totally sucks. Lola has one chance to change her destiny, from undertaker to classically trained musician. Can she win a scholarship to London, before her hearing disappears completely?

title: Can You Hear a Rainbow? The Story of a Deaf Boy Named Chris
author: Jamee Riggio Heelan

title: Can You Hear Me Now?
author: TIffany Dawn Kohnen
summary: Do you want to be a better ally to people of the deaf community? Reading this book is a great first step. Have you seen someone struggling with accessibility and wanted to help, but were afraid of offending them? Do you want to help your child who is struggling with their hearing loss? Do you want to get to know your Deaf neighbor or coworker (Captial D means something, learn more in the book)? Can You Hear Me Now? tackles fourteen different subjects and strategies meant to educate you about this disability so you can be there for the Deaf and hard of hearing.

title: Carl Enters a Contest
author: Erin Savory
summary: Carl is Deaf. He cannot hear, but he can use his other senses to help him do things instead. So when Carl enters a baking contest, he is ready to be the best baker he can be! Follow along as Carl works hard to compete in a baking competition in the fiction book Carl Enters a Contest!

title: Carl Goes on a Field Trip
author: Erin Savory
summary: Carl is Deaf. He cannot hear, but he can learn anything. So when his class goes on a field trip to the museum, Carl is ready to show what he knows! Follow along as Carl stands up for himself so he can participate during a field trip in the fiction book Carl Goes on a Field Trip. Carl was first introduced in our Katie Can series, based on our best-selling book of the same title. Continue following along on Carl’s journey in the new and inclusive Carl Can series.

title: Carl Makes a Plan
author: Erin Savory
summary: Carl is Deaf. He cannot hear, but there are many things that Carl can do. So when his friend is about to have a birthday, Carl plans the entire party! Follow along as Carl plans an entire birthday party for his friend using methods that suit his needs, like using sign language in the fiction book Carl Makes a Plan!

title: Carl Writes a Letter
author: Erin Savory
summary: Carl is Deaf. When he notices that his library does not have any books with Deaf characters, he writes a letter to make a positive change that’s inclusive of the asl community. Follow along as Carl writes a letter to help get more Deaf representation in books in the inspirational fiction book Carl Writes a Letter!

title: Carson Can Hear!
author: Cindy Dean
summary: Carson is a joyful little boy with a big smile, but sometimes he has trouble hearing the world around him. Follow Carson’s heartwarming journey as he discovers the magic of hearing aids and learns to hear his family, friends, and all the sounds that make life wonderful. From the laughter of playtime to the whispers of bedtime stories, Carson shows that with love, support, and a little technology, anything is possible.Perfect for families, teachers, and classrooms, Carson Can Hear! helps children understand hearing loss and celebrate the beauty of inclusion. This cheerful story offers encouragement, representation, and hope to kids with hearing aids and their friends who want to learn more.

title: Carson Gets His FM System!
author: Cindy Dean
summary: Carson is starting preschool with excitement and a little uncertainty. His hearing aids help him every day — but in a noisy classroom, it’s still hard to keep up. That’s when his teacher introduces something new: an FM system! With a tiny microphone clipped to her shirt, Carson can hear her voice loud and clear, no matter how busy or noisy the room gets. From circle time and block play to raising his hand with confidence, Carson discovers how this small device makes a big difference. And when a funny lunchtime surprise has everyone laughing (spaghetti, anyone?), Carson realizes his FM system isn’t just helpful — it’s pretty cool! Carson Gets His FM System! is a heartwarming and educational picture book for children ages 3–6, families, and classrooms. With playful illustrations, gentle rhymes, and a joyful story, this book helps kids understand assistive technology, celebrates inclusion, and shows that every child deserves to be heard.

title: Carson Loves Speech Therapy!
author: Cindy Dean
summary: Carson is a joyful little boy with bright blue hearing aids and an even brighter smile. Today is a special day—speech therapy day! With the help of his many kind therapists, Carson practices sounds, plays games, and discovers the joy of finding his voice. This heartwarming story celebrates the hard work, courage, and progress children make in speech therapy, showing young readers that learning can be fun, playful, and full of encouragement. Perfect for families, classrooms, and therapists, Carson Loves Speech Therapy! is a gentle and empowering picture book that honors every child’s journey toward communication.

title: Catch a Star: Shining through Adversity to Become a Champion
author: Tamika Catchings, Ken Petersen

title: Cattywampus
author: Ash Van Otterloo
summary: In the town of Howler's Hollow, conjuring magic is strictly off-limits. Only nothing makes Delpha McGill's skin crawl more than rules. So when she finds her family's secret book of hexes, she's itching to use it to banish her mama's money troubles. She just has to keep it quieter than a church mouse -- not exactly Delpha's specialty.Trouble is, Katybird Hearn is hankering to get her hands on the spell book, too. The daughter of a rival witching family, Katy has reasons of her own for wanting to learn forbidden magic, and she's not going to let an age-old feud or Delpha's contrary ways stop her. But their quarrel accidentally unleashes a hex so heinous it resurrects a graveyard full of angry Hearn and McGill ancestors bent on total destruction. If Delpha and Katy want to reverse the spell in time to save everyone in the Hollow from rampaging zombies, they'll need to mend fences and work together.

title: Celestial Monsters
author: Aiden Thomas
summary: Teo never thought he could be a Hero. Now, he doesn’t have a choice. The sun is gone, the Obsidian gods have been released from their prison, and chaos and destruction are wreaking havoc on Reino del Sol. All because Teo refused to sacrifice a fellow semidiós during the Sunbearer Trials. With the world plunged into perpetual night, Teo, his crush Aurelio, and his best friend Niya must journey to the dark wilderness of Los Restos, battling vicious monsters while dealing with guilt, trauma, and a (very distracting) burgeoning romance between Teo and Aurelio. Determined to rescue the captured semidioses and retrieve the Sol Stone, the trio races against the clock to return Sol and their protective light. With it, order can be restored. The future of the whole world is in their hands.

title: Changes for Julie
author: Megan McDonald
summary: When Julie runs for student body president, the other students are put off by her choice for vice president, a deaf student named Joy.

title: Charlie and Frog
author: Karen Kane
summary: All Charlie Tickler wants is for his parents to listen. Charlie's parents have left him (again). This time they are off to South Africa to help giant golden moles. And Charlie? He's been dumped with his TV-obsessed grandparents. Lonely and curious, Charlie heads into the village of Castle-on-the-Hudson, where a frightened old woman gives him a desperate message-in sign language. When she suddenly disappears, Charlie is determined to find answers. All Francine (aka Frog) Castle wants is to be the world's greatest detective. Frog, who is Deaf, would rather be solving crimes than working at the Flying Hands Cafe. When Charlie Tickler walks into the cafe looking for help, Frog jumps at the chance to tackle a real-life case. Together, Charlie and Frog set out to decipher a series of clues and uncover the truth behind the missing woman's mysterious message. Charlie needs to learn American Sign Language (fast) to keep up with quick-witted Frog. And Frog needs to gather her detective know-how (now) to break the case before it's too late.

title: Charlie and Fuzz
author: Carl Jones
summary: Charlie loves experiencing the wonders of the world through their four super senses. Unable to hear, Charlie doesn't let that stop them and instead focuses on all the delightful sights, smells, tastes, and feelings they encounter. While out running errands with their mum, Charlie meets a new fuzzy friend that's deaf just like them.

title: Charlie and Fuzz: A voyage through the stars
author: Carl Jones
summary: Join Charlie and their enchanting new friend, Fuzz, on a magical journey across the cosmos in this heartwarming tale of friendship and discovery. In this sequel, Fuzz invites Charlie to explore the wonders of the universe. Together, they sign and laugh their way past twinkling stars and swirling galaxies. Charlie, who is deaf, shows that adventures are for everyone and with Fuzz by their side, there's no limit to the wonders they can explore.

title: Christmas
author: Sophia Day
summary: Join our MVP Kids in Celebrate! Christmas as they perform in their community’s annual Christmas pageant! Celebrated around the world, Christmas is an integral part of the holiday season for families of diverse cultural and religious backgrounds. Through their playful sets and biblical script, our MVP Kids teach us the central story to the Christian celebration of Christmas.

title: Christmas Signs
author: Kristi Mumm
summary: This Christmas-themed picture book brings holiday cheer and hands-on learning together in one magical package! A perfect introduction to American Sign Language (ASL), this festive book is designed for classrooms and families alike. Each sign is paired with a playful holiday poem and vibrant illustrations that make learning ASL fun, memorable, and full of seasonal spirit. From decorating the tree to sharing cookies and kindness, children will explore the joy of the holidays while discovering new signs along the way. Whether you're snuggled up at home or celebrating in the classroom, this heartwarming book encourages inclusion, communication, and connection.

title: Christmas Stories: The Adventures of Andy and Aya
author: Darry Borisova
series: The Adventures of Andy and Aya
summary: The Adventures of Andy and Aya book series tells the story of a deaf girl called Dara, whose love brings her favorite toys to life - the giraffe Andy and the doll Aya. This book is about their special Christmas adventures together. Five stories full of love and the little miracles of everyday life which make such a big difference in the girl’s world.The author of the book, Darry Borisova, established the Andy and Aya Academy for Children from the Deaf Community in Bulgaria in 2017, where she works with deaf children and their families. Darry herself is a CODA (Child of Deaf Adults) and a member of the Deaf community, raised with the challenges of Deaf people in Bulgaria. She is also a qualified Deaf therapist and Sign Language interpreter. The book series aims at raising awareness about deafness and promoting accessible deaf education throughout the world. All proceeds of the book sales go towards supporting the development of the Academy and its efforts to offer a wider range of high quality affordable educational opportunities for deaf children in Bulgaria.

title: Cicada City: A Bug Club Story
author: Gina Gallois
summary: The Bug Club is on the hunt for cicadas! Jaylen, Hyacinth, and Sakura have big plans this summer. What fascinating facts will they discover about cicadas? How far will their imagination take them? Will you join the fun? This book is 32 pages of fun facts and inclusive, imaginative play for insect-loving kids 3-8 and their grownups.

title: Clive is a Librarian
author: Jessica Spanyol
summary: What will Clive be today? Follow Clive and his friends as they explore the many things a librarian does in a day. Role-play is crucial to a child's positive development, boosting language and social skills, and building self-confidence.

title: Clive is a Teacher
author: Jessica Spanyol
summary: What will Clive be today? Follow Clive and his friends as they explore the many things a teacher does in a day. Role-play is crucial to a child's positive development, boosting language and social skills, and building self-confidence.

title: Clive is a Waiter
author: Jessica Spanyol
summary: What will Clive be today? Follow Clive and his friends as they explore the many things a waiter does in a day. Role-play is crucial to a child's positive development, boosting language and social skills, and building self-confidence.

title: Colors with KJ
author: Kentrell Martin, Kasey Martin
summary: KJ is Shelly’s Deaf baby brother, and he communicates using American Sign Language (ASL). KJ’s series teaches signs for emotions, objects, food, numbers, places, people, pets, activities and so much more!

title: Completely Emme: A Cerebral Palsy Story
author: Justine Green

title: Completely Toby: A Down Syndrome Story
author: Justine Green

title: Cookie Friends: Our Friend Luis Is Deaf
author: Kirsten McDonald

title: Count with KJ
author: Kentrell Martin, Kasey Martin
summary: Count with KJ is the 10th book in the Shelly's Adventures series. This is the second fun-filled book inThe Shelly’s Adventures collection from KJ. KJ is Shelly’s Deaf baby brother, and he communicates using American Sign Language (ASL). In this book, KJ will teach the readers the signs for the ASL numbers one through 10. KJ’s series teaches signs for emotions, objects, food, numbers, places, people, pets, activities and so much more!

title: Dad and Me in the Morning
author: Patricia Lakin

title: Daisy Doo All the Sounds She Knew
author: Daisy Kent

title: Daniel McCrew and You - All About My Accommodations; Hearing Loss Help for Students, Families and Teachers
author: Catherine Madonia and Vielka Montout

title: Deaf Blind Champion: A True Story of Hope, Inspiration, and Excellence in Sport and Life.
author: Kevin Frost

title: Deaf Ghosts at Eerie Elementary - True Biz!
author: Litterah C. Atwork (Pen Name)/ Deborah Love

title: Deaf in America: Voices from a Culture
author: Carol A. Padden, Tom L. Humphries

title: Deaf People Can Do Anything Except Hear: An American History of the Deaf
author: Eric Brody

title: Deaf Republic: Poems
author: Ilya Kaminsky
summary: Deaf Republic opens in an occupied country in a time of political unrest. When soldiers breaking up a protest kill a deaf boy, Petya, the gunshot becomes the last thing the citizens hear―they all have gone deaf, and their dissent becomes coordinated by sign language. The story follows the private lives of townspeople encircled by public violence: a newly married couple, Alfonso and Sonya, expecting a child; the brash Momma Galya, instigating the insurgency from her puppet theater; and Galya’s girls, heroically teaching signing by day and by night luring soldiers one by one to their deaths behind the curtain. At once a love story, an elegy, and an urgent plea, Ilya Kaminsky’s long-awaited Deaf Republic confronts our time’s vicious atrocities and our collective silence in the face of them.

title: Deaf Utopia: A Memoir - and a Love Letter to a Way of Life
author: Nyle Dimarco

title: Deafinitely Awesome: The Story of Acorn
author: Mary L. Motley

title: Dear Steve, You're Going Deaf
author: Steven Louis DiCesare
summary: What is it like to go deaf? A humorous, real and at times harrowing exploration of one man's lifelong journey into silence. The entertaining, true short stories describe adventures in communication and music as well as Deaf culture and the cochlear implant. The author chronicles his experiences navigating the setbacks of hearing loss, sharing his darkest moments as well as his triumphs. In many ways, it is a story of survival. The result is a remarkable exercise in self-discovery and self-healing. The stories provide life-affirming lessons, such as the importance of altering our mindset in the face of adversity. Complete with clever and impactful illustrations, this book is not only for the Deaf and people with hearing impairments but for anyone struggling through a frightening or life-changing event.

title: Defeat Disobedience
author: Sophia Day
summary: How can our MVP Kids overcome disobedience? Learning to obey parents, teachers and coaches can be difficult. Join Aanya, Sarah, and Ezekiel as they Defeat Disobedience and begin to obey right away. Grow alongside our MVP Kids as you read these short stories on becoming obedient.

title: Demystifying Disability: What to Know, What to Say, and How to Be an Ally
author: Emily Ladau

title: Diary of a Hard of Hearing Kid
author: Isaiah John Baier

title: Different: A Great Thing to Be
author: Heather Avis

title: Disability Visibility: 17 First-person Stories for Today: Adapted for Young Adults
author: Alice Wong (Editor)

title: Do Pebbles Eat Chili? and other outlandish poems
author: Jay Miletsky

title: Dr. Ochoa’s Stellar World: Arts: A Bilingual STEAM book
author: Ellen Ochoa
summary: Joining Dr. Ochoa’s Stellar World series’ is the artistic “A” in STEAM, Dr. Ochoa’s Stellar World: Arts! Little ones will be guided through an out-of-this-world journey through the arts, from acting, painting, dancing, and much more. Ellen Ochoa will introduce them to all the art forms in which they can show their curiosity and creativity. Along the way, your future artistic trailblazers will learn how teamwork and innovation can lead to new showstopping adventures! Parents will adore this bilingual English-Spanish STEAM Board Books as it introduces little ones to all the extraordinary ways their passions for art and science can be combined into one.

title: Dr. Ochoa’s Stellar World: Engineering: A Bilingual STEAM book
author: Ellen Ochoa

title: Dr. Sylver and the Library of Everything
author: Paul Kercal
summary: Jamie Meek has a quiet life, if you ignore the fact that his Dad recently left his Mum taking all the family savings, and he has few friends to speak with, oh, and his sister is the school's biggest pest (female category) while he is the target of the bullying intentions of a gang of equal pests (male category). At least he knows his place in the world, until Dr Sylver the school librarian gets orders to open up a new world to him; a world that moves sometimes faster sometimes slower; a world with unexpected friends and enemies; a world with more at stake than the usual business of surviving at school or improving scores on his PS2. But how on earth did Jamie get roped into that football competition.

title: Dr. Sylver and the Repository of the Past
author: Paul Kercal
summary: Jamie Meek thought he understood life. School was dull, he was bullied and his family - what was left of it - argued...Then he met Dr Sylver, a school librarian who seemed to know, well, everything...His world turned upside down. He finds himself in the Repository of the Past, a building made of stone, jewel and whisper. Once inside he has to cope without the help of his three friends, without the help of Dr Sylver. Look out Jamie, to follow Dr Sylver is to court death!

title: Dr. Sylver and the Tapestry of TIme
author: Paul Kercal
summary: Dr Sylver is an angel; in disguise, she works in a regular school library. In the final volume of the trilogy, everything leads on towards a series of painful betrayals. One school's future lies in jeopardy but the crucial issue is far more cosmic: the dark powers desire to lever off certain people's d-i-y spiritual warfare to subvert time itself.

title: Dreams Take Flight: The Story of Deaf Pilot Nellie Zabel Willhite
author: Brittany Richman

title: Ears to Hear
author: Kriss King
summary: Join Doctor Zippy, the encouraging and uplifting horse, as he interacts with his animal patients in the second book of the Just Keep Zippin' series, "Ears to Hear." Doc Zippy sees Boom Boom, an elephant who needs hearing aids. Zippy finds ways to help Boom Boom get over his fears of being different in school. Zippy also helps Boom Boom picture the new world of confidence and activities that he’ll be able to enjoy with his new ears. Author Kriss includes personal examples from her own lifelong profound hearing loss. Illustrator daughter Angel (and human hearing aid with ironically supersonic hearing of her own) captures imagery and concepts from real family & school situations. Zippy’s insights and “Hoovescriptions” guide his patients through common worries and challenges faced by young children. New ways of thinking, encouragement, and solutions empower each patient in the series to conquer the challenges, and to celebrate the positive impacts they can have on others.

title: Ears To Hear: Caden Gets Cochlear Implants
author: Jillian Bueller
summary: Caden is getting cochlear implants and Cassie, his sister, has many questions about her brothers new way of being able to hear. Luckily for Cassie, her babysitter, Emily, has a brother with cochlear implants too! Join Cassie and Emily as they chat through some of the curiosities Cassie comes up with.

title: Ears To Hear: Caden Gets Processors
author: Jillian Bueller
summary: Today is the day! Caden is getting his processors and Cassie is so excited for Caden to hear. But how does Cadens new way of hearing work? Will Caden be able to talk? What does a processor look like? Come along with Emily and Cassie as they find out about Caden’s new processors!

title: Echo: The Saga of Maya Lopez
author: David Mack

title: Emma & Egor: First Words Book 1
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: You are your child’s first teacher and the beginning the journey of communication with sign language is intended to help infants and toddlers express their wants and needs before they are ready to communication verbally. It’s easy to build an early sign language vocabulary with First Words. Clear illustrations and descriptions teach parents, caregivers and educators the signs and words most often used to communicate with very young children.

title: Emma & Egor: First Words Book 2
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: You are your child’s first teacher. Are you ready to embarking on a fulfilling journey of communicating with sign language? Learning sign language helps infants and toddlers express their wants and needs before they are ready to communication verbally. It’s easy to continue to build an early sign language vocabulary with First Words Book 2. Clear illustrations and descriptions teach parents, caregivers and educators additional signs and words most often used to communicate with very young children.

title: Emma & Egor: Get Ready for School
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: Every day Emma, Egor and Eli Get Ready for School! They wake-up, choose what they'll wear, what to pack in their lunches and make decisions about what they'll put in their backpacks, all while Signing Exact English. Each word in the story is accompanied by its sign enabling children and adults to share a story and learn new words. Emma and Egor make reading and signing easy and enjoyable for deaf/hard-of-hearing and hearing children.

title: Emma & Egor: How are you Feeling?
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: Emma, Egor and their new friend Eli learn to sign the words for different feelings children may encounter every day in school, while playing or when visiting the doctor. Each illustration draws from a real-life situation that children have the ability to respond to in sign language describing how the character might feel. Most schools are playing a large role in in addressing social-emotional challenges that may arise when children are unable to identify or express how they feel. This can result in behavioral issues, especially if a child is non-verbal or unable to communicate verbally. This book, along with the sign language flashcards, "FaceCards" and playing cards help teachers and young children tackle different types of emotions they may encounter every day

title: Emma & Egor: Learn to Sign their ABCs
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: Emma, Egor and their new friend Eli make learning to sign the alphabet simple, straightforward and fun for deaf/hard-of-hearing and hearing children. Each letter of the alphabet is clearly signed by Emma, Egor or Eli encouraging children to learn and sign their ABC's. Emma and Egor books were created for all children and their families. Emma and Egor help teach the basics of Signing Exact English using simple stories and situations using full sentences and grammar. Learn to sign numbers, the alphabet, colors, shapes and everyday activities like getting ready for school.

title: Emma & Egor: Learn to Sign Numbers
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: Learning the signs for numbers is easy with Emma and Egor. Deaf/hard-of-hearing and hearing children can help Emma and Egor learn to count. Each number is accompanied by its corresponding sign making it easy for children and adults alike to build their Signing Exact English vocabulary.

title: Emma & Egor: Shapes and Colors
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: Learning the signs for basic shapes and colors is easy with Emma and Egor. Deaf/hard-of-hearing and hearing children can help Egor find his black horse or point out Emma's yellow triangle. Each word is accompanied by its corresponding sign making it easy for children and adults alike to build their Signing Exact English vocabulary.

title: Emma & Egor: What Day is it?
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: What day is it? Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday! Learning the signs for the days and the months of the year is easy with Emma, Egor and Eli. Deaf/hard-of-hearing and hearing children can help these adorable children learn these important concepts. Each day and month is accompanied by its corresponding sign making it easy for children and adults alike to build their Signing Exact English vocabulary.

title: Emma & Egor: What is the Weather Like Today?
author: Stacy Eldred
series: Emma and Egor Learn Sign Language
summary: Is it Winter, Spring, Summer or Fall? What is the weather like today? Rainy, sunny, windy, snowy? What do you do on a rainy summer day? Learning the signs for the weather, seasons and activities is easy with Emma, Egor and Eli. Deaf/hard-of-hearing and hearing children can help these adorable children learn these important concepts. Each season and type of weather is accompanied by its corresponding sign making it easy for children and adults alike to build their Signing Exact English vocabulary

title: Emma Everyday: A Trip to Grandma's
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma and her brother are staying at their grandmother's house for a full week; she has packed her favorite stuffed toy and the recharger for her cochlear implant, but she is a little worried about feeling homesick--but with her grandmother keeping her busy, and the help of a new deaf friend named Nick, the week flies by.

title: Emma Everyday: Camping Fun
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma, who is deaf and uses sign language to communicate, enjoys a summer camping trip with her family. Includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and sign language guide.

title: Emma Everyday: Cat Care
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma loves animals, and she can't wait to foster a cat! But what will happen when she brings the cat home? Will Emma and the cat get along? Will Emma's fish and the cat get along? Emma is Deaf and often uses sign language to communicate, and this early chapter book includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and a sign language guide.

title: Emma Everyday: Cooking with Grandma
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma loves spending time with her Grandma. And it's even more fun when it includes cooking! Emma is finally old enough to help make a birthday meal for her dad. But will everything turn out? Emma is Deaf and often uses sign language to communicate, and this early chapter book includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and a sign language guide.

title: Emma Everyday: Crazy for Apples
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Fall is Emma's favorite season. She loves the weather, the leaves, and most of all, the apples! Every fall, Emma's dad takes Emma and her best friend, Izzie, to the apple orchard. And every year they pick dozens of apples so they can make apple pies, applesauce, apple tarts, and other apple treats. But this year, things don't go as planned at the orchard. Follow Emma and Izzie on their apple adventure in this early chapter book from the Emma Every Day series.

title: Emma Everyday: Dentist Day
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma is worried about her dentist appointment. What if she has a cavity? What if she didn't floss enough? Thankfully Emma has a good team of people to help her feel better. Emma is Deaf and often uses sign language to communicate, and this early chapter book includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and a sign language guide.

title: Emma Everyday: Dog Watch
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma loves animals and can't wait to babysit a neighbor's dog for the weekend. But Emma has no idea how hard it is to take care of a dog! Hopefully Emma can keep things under control and still have some fun! Emma is Deaf and often uses sign language to communicate, and this early chapter book includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and a sign language guide.

title: Emma Everyday: Everyday Fun!
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Join Emma on her fun everyday adventures! Experience the world like she does as a Deaf child with a cochlear implant. Four stories in one book will help readers understand different aspects of Emma's life as they learn ASL fingerspelling and find inspiration in her upbeat attitude. Includes Crazy for Apples, Going on a Field Trip, Party Problems and Tap Dance Troubles.

title: Emma Everyday: First Flight
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma and her family are taking a trip. And this trip includes going on a plane! Emma is excited about flying on the plane, but she is also nervous. Will Emma like flying? Emma is Deaf and often uses sign language to communicate, and this early chapter book includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and a sign language guide

title: Emma Everyday: Friendship Goals
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma, who is deaf and wears a cochlear implant, loves playing soccer with her friends Izzie and Chen, but when Izzie gets discouraged and wants to quit, Emma and Chen take the time to show her that improvement comes through practice.

title: Emma Everyday: Going on a Field Trip
author: C.L. Reid
summary: It's the first field trip of the year! Emma's class is headed to a history museum. Field trips are supposed to be fun, but how much fun can you have looking at old things all day? Leave it to Emma to find the fun in everything, including history, in this early chapter book from the Emma Every Day series.

title: Emma Everyday: Party Problems
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma is excited about Izzie's birthday party. But she's also nervous. Is her dress too fancy? Will she know anyone else at the party? Did she buy the right gift? Will Emma's worries ruin her chance to have fun? Find out how Emma handles her party problems in this early chapter book from the Emma Every Day series.

title: Emma Everyday: Pottery Project
author: C.L. Reid
summary: It's time to try something new, and Emma loves art. But Emma has never tried pottery before. It's a lot harder than it looks! Will Emma keep trying or give up before her project is done? Emma is Deaf and often uses sign language to communicate, and this early chapter book includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and a sign language guide.

title: Emma Everyday: Roller Skating Worries
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma's best friend Izzie loves roller skating, and she is going to teach Emma; Emma is worried about falling and hurting herself and her nerves make her wobbly--but Izzie is sure that listening to their favorite music will help her relax and forget her nerves.

title: Emma Everyday: Swimming Struggle
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma and her best friend, Izzie, try out for the swim team. Izzie doesn't make it, but Emma does. Will Emma still want to be on the team without her best friend? Emma is Deaf and often uses sign language to communicate, and this early chapter book includes an ASL fingerspelling chart and a sign language guide.

title: Emma Everyday: Tap Dance Troubles
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Click. Clack. Tap, tap, tap. Emma just started dance lessons and is determined to learn the routine perfectly. But dance isn't as easy as she had hoped. Thankfully Emma doesn't give up easily. Emma proves that hard work and practice will take those tap dance troubles away in this early chapter book from the Emma Every Day series.

title: Emma Everyday: The Big Bike
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma gets a bigger bike and can't wait to ride it, but when she falls off she's too scared to try it anymore. When her best friend, Izzie, comes over for a bike ride, Emma uses her old bike. Emma needs to practice on her new big bike and gain confidence to ride again--even if it is scary.

title: Emma Everyday: The Lemonade Stand
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Best friends Izzie and Emma, who is deaf, open a lemonade stand and learn how to run a business. Includes words in sign language, a glossary, content-related questions, and writing prompts.

title: Emma Everyday: The Special Pumpkin
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma loves Halloween! She has the perfect costume that works well with her cochlear implant. But she doesn’t have a pumpkin to carve. Emma searches for the perfect pumpkin, but she soon discovers that perfect isn't important in this early chapter book.

title: Emma Everyday: The Speedy Sled
author: C.L. Reid
summary: After an epic snowfall, Emma is ready to try sledding. But it's a lot scarier than she thought it would be! Emma wears a cochlear implant because she's Deaf, and she's worried it might fall off. Emma must face her fears to discover new fun in this early chapter book.

title: Emma Everyday: The Writing Contest
author: C.L. Reid
summary: Emma enters a writing contest. She loves writing and is confident that she will win. But when that doesn't happen, will Emma want to keep writing?

title: Enchanting Songs for Silent Sirens
author: Laura Greenwood
summary: Melody loves to sing, which is a bit of a problem when doing that could enchant everyone around her with her siren magic. Instead, she doesn't talk to anyone about her love of Christmas carols and keeps her powers a secret. Until she meets Josh and the two of them form a connection deeper than any she's ever experienced before. Can he help her find her voice? Enchanting Songs For Silent Sirens is a holiday story and part of the Obscure Academy series. It's a light-hearted m/f paranormal romance with a deaf love interest. It is Melody and Josh's complete story.

title: EPHPHATHA: Growing up profoundly Deaf and not dumb in the hearing world: A basketball player's transformational journey to the ivy league
author: Dr. Thomas M. Caulfield

title: Everly's Ears: A talk of Kindness and Inclusivity
author: Claire Thompson

title: Every Body, A Celebration of Diverse Abilities
author: Shelley Rotner

title: Every Hero Has a Story: Hearing Loss
author: Abigail Arndt

title: Every Sunday
author: Och Gonzalez
summary: Kuya Berto, the ice cream vendor, is really cool! I want to be friends with him but I don't know how to talk to him. He can't hear and I also don't know sign language. How can we be friends?

title: Everyday I Am Just Deaf: Life in a Hearing World with Deaf Queen Boss
author: Kellina Powell

title: Extraordinary Jordyn and her Bionic Ears
author: Jasmine Simmons

title: Faith's Stress Rehearsal
author: Sophia Day
summary: Faith is always full of energy and loves to say “yes” to new opportunities, but she’s setting herself up for a disaster! Over-committed and stressed out, Faith must learn to prioritize, delegate, and practice self-care. Will she be able to manage the demands without breaking her commitments? Can you? Understand stress and learn to balance work and rest with our MVP Kids.

title: Falling into Fear
author: Anne Colledge
summary: A story featuring a deaf young girl who has to leave her home for a while, to live with her grandparents, because her mother is seriously ill. She experiences disconcerting time-slip adventures, but gradually she learns not to be frightened by them, but rather to learn to cope with the difficulties of her real life.

title: Family Signs
author: Kristi Mumm
summary: This family-themed picture book is an interactive read-aloud for classrooms or families curious about learning sign language. With kid-friendly explanations, colorful illustrations, and playful poems, each sign celebrates the love and connection within a family. All signs were created with a Deaf consultant to ensure they are accurate and well represented. Each book is dedicated to someone who chooses to make the world a better place—because learning to communicate is one small way we can all help. Join us by the family tree, where love grows and everyone can learn to communicate in a whole new way!

title: Feel the Sound
author: Evangeline Duran Fuentes
summary: Bobby valued having a best friend above everything else, but friendship with the new boy would challenge him in ways he’d never faced before. Learning a new language would prove to be a difficult task, especially while coping with a pushy neighborhood girl. Will Bobby have the patience to overcome all these challenges, in order to have the best friend he dreamed of?

title: Feelings
author: Sophia Day
summary: Join our MVP Kids in a page-flipping, mood-flopping discovery of feelings. Children will learn to name and express emotions, gaining self-awareness and coping skills as they experience the daily ups and downs of childhood.

title: Fighting for YES!: The Story of Disability Rights Activist Judith Heumann
author: Maryann Cocca-Leffler

title: Firefly and the Quest of the Black Squirrel
author: J. H. Sweet

title: First Friends: Numbers
author: MK Smith Despres
summary: At drop off there's one kiss goodbye, one hug hello, and ten waves goodbye when it's time to go. Count through the day from playtime to naptime at daycare!

title: First Friends: Shapes
author: MK Smith Despres
summary: In our day care class, it's time to play. Let's find shapes along the way! There are triangles, squares, diamonds, and other shapes in the paintings, blocks, and dolls. What other shapes can you find?

title: Flying to the Fire
author: Elyse Salpeter
summary: An evil black mass has burst through Danny Anderson’s nightmares to attack him in broad daylight. If it weren’t for his birds, he would have died. Danny soon realizes that this monster is not coming for him, but for his little sister, Katie...because while he looks up to a place of light and peace, Katie looks down into realms of darkness and cruelty. Time is running out. The monster grows stronger with each attempt to steal Katie away to these dark dimensions, forcing Danny into the fight of his life to save his sister--before she is lost to him forever.

title: Flying to the Light
author: Elyse Salpeter
summary: Seventeen year old Michael Anderson and his Deaf kid brother, Danny, find themselves in mortal danger after their parents are kidnapped. Michael discovers Danny has a special gift—he knows what happens after a person dies—and now others want to know too. The brothers must outwit and outrun Samuel Herrington, a lethal biophysicist, the FBI, and even fellow Americans in a harrowing cross-country chase, because whoever gets to Danny first will have the power to rule the world.

title: For Deaf Kids: Know Your Language Rights
author: Avril Hertneky

title: Four Days With Kenny Tedford: Life Through the Eyes of a Child Trapped in a Partially Blind & Deaf Man's Body
author: Paul Smith & Kenny Tedford, Jr.

title: From Rejection to Love: The True Story of a Deaf Woman Who Overcame Abuse and Neglect
author: Avril Hertneky
summary: As a Deaf woman growing up in today's society, I've faced numerous obstacles, hardships, and struggles. Born in Toronto and raised in Brampton, Ontario, Canada, my childhood was marked by turmoil, and my sense of safety was constantly jeopardized. The physical, mental, and emotional pain I endured still lingers, a constant reminder of my traumatic past. Despite this, I've found purpose in life as a mother, determined to shield my children from the suffering I experienced. I'm compelled to share my story, a difficult and biased truth shaped by my family's upbringing and culture. By exposing the skeletons of my past, I hope to release the pain and find freedom, not just for myself, but for others who have endured similar childhood horrors. My book aims to be a balm for those who have suffered, a reminder that no one should have to endure such trauma. If you know someone struggling, please encourage them to seek professional help and involve the authorities to ensure protection and safety, especially for children.

title: From there to HEAR... How I got my Cochlear Implant
author: Emily Jenkins

title: Flying Colors
author: Sophia Day
summary: Join our MVP Kids in Celebrate! Flying Colors during playtime in the beautiful outdoors of Patel Daycare. Their imaginations soar as they learn about colors from the birds and butterflies flying and fluttering around them.

title: Gabby Bears With Embarrassment
author: Sophia Day
summary: Gabby’s camp retreat got off to an embarrassing start! Her backpack burst open and her personal things flew out for all to see. How will she face her peers again? Understand embarrassment with our MVP Kids as they discover how perspective, humor, and compassion keep embarrassment from becoming an obstacle to fun and friendship.

title: Gale and Oscar Learn Sign Language
author: Nightingale Baby
summary: Sign language has stopped billions of tantrums in their tracks, quelled millions of meltdowns, and soothed upset babies around the world... but it also helps us understand and connect with our pre-verbal children in a deeper and more meaningful way. So, we created this book with the hopes that it will make communicating with your little one(s) just a little bit easier and a lot more fun. We hope you read this story of Gale and the Baby with your kids, learn to sign together, and have some fun!

title: Garland of Lights: A Diwali Story
author: Chitra Soundar

title: Genesis
author: Helen Dennis
summary: When a mystery teenage boy emerges from the River Thames drenched, distressed and unable to remember anything about himself, he becomes the focus of worldwide media speculation. Unable to communicate, the River Boy is given paper and a pencil and begins to scribble. Soon a symbol emerges, but the boy has no idea why he has drawn it even though it's the only clue to the mystery of his identity. As the boy begins to build a new life under a new name, the hunt for his real identity begins. A hunt which will lead him on a dangerous QUEST that he has only one year to complete…

title: George Is Not Only Deaf
author: Megan Wofsy
summary: George is a six year old Deaf boy. We see George hanging out with both his Deaf and hearing friends and how he learns to communicate with them.

title: Get Your Elbow Off the Horn: Stories through the Years
author: Jack R. Gannon

title: Girls of the World: Doing More than Ever Before
author: Linsey Davis and Michael Tyler

title: Grandma's Ears are Broken
author: Pat Goldys and Marlene Shipley Everhardt

title: Guardian
author: StarAndrea
summary: The mid-term break at Smith Valley means that Kae can finally get some work done. Since Hope has managed to keep herself out of trouble for several days in a row, he accepts a reconnaissance mission to Sirian space and a followup undercover operation on Earth. The first person he encounters isn't a person at all, but the android avatar used by the Smith Valley AI: also undercover, and surprisingly willing to help him. KERI claims to be bored, and it turns out she isn't kidding. She sets herself up as his "security coordinator" and keeps an eye on anyone trying to sabotage him without being asked. She offers her opinion freely, and it's surprisingly helpful: between his family's observations and her speculation, they make Kae's company contacts work for them. While Kae and KERI pursue the Sirians' true purpose on Earth, Michiko and Jenni are on their way back to Smith Valley. The nearest transportation hub happens to be Roris' homeworld, so they get him to give them a tour during their layover. When the three of them look up Kechana to see if she'll introduce them to her dolphin friends, it turns out that she already has a visitor of her own. Hope is on Alandia, where she and Kechana are making the most of the vacation they have left.

title: H is for Hormones: An A-to-Z Guide to Your Changing Body
author: Dr. Tiffany Panko, Dr. Corrine Occhino, Susan Rizzo
summary: You likely know someone who has chronic unexplained vaginal pain (or vulvodynia), painful periods from endometriosis, or recurrent yeast infections. Maybe this is you. These things are usually not openly talked about... especially if you are a teenage girl. The topic may be taboo or the information goes over your head. We are a team of deaf and hearing, sign-fluent women that came together in our shared goal of changing this! This ABC book is a women’s health book with feedback and input of deaf middle school girls, with the vision of it being accompanied by a video series of deaf women in STEM translating the book.

title: Haben: The Deafblind Woman Who Conquered Harvard Law
author: Haben Girma

title: Halloween Signs
author: Kristi Mumm
summary: This Halloween-themed picture book is the perfect introduction for kids! With kid-friendly explanations and vibrant illustrations, learning ASL has never been so exciting. A wise first grader once said, “Everyone should learn sign language because it stretches your brain and you can make new friends!” Each sign is paired with a fun poem to get everyone in the spooky spirit. Journey through a haunted house and discover the signs that bring the story to life! Great for classrooms, homes, and gift-giving, this book makes learning ASL accessible, memorable, and a whole lot of fun!

title: Hand Talk: Reni Goes to School
author: Barbara Champion
summary: Hand Talk: Reni Goes to School is the story of a young boy who is apprehensive about going to school because of his difficulty communicating. Reni learns how to use sign language and discovers that school can be fun. He shows readers how to make signs to identify his teacher, his classes, the supplies he uses, and more. The story is illustrated with fun and colorful drawings. The second half of the book contains photographs of the author’s students demonstrating how to make more than two dozen signs in American Sign Language, from “library,” “book,” and “recess” to “bus,” “calendar,” and “gym.”

title: Hands of My Father: A Hearing Boy, His Deaf Parents, and the Language of Love
author: Myron Uhlberg

title: Happy Holi!: Celebrating the Festival of Colors
author: Chitra Soundar

title: HAWKEYE: THE SAGA OF BARTON AND BISHOP
author: Matt Fraction

title: He Is Not Me: A Deaf Childhood, A Hearing Adulthood, One Life
author: Stuart McNaughton

title: Hear & Beyond: Live Skillfully with Hearing Loss
author: Shari Eberts and Gael Hannan

title: Hear Again: Back to Life with a Cochlear Implant
author: Arlene Romoff

title: Helen's Big World: The Life of Helen Keller
author: Doreen Rappaport

title: Hello, World! I'm a Programmer
author: Sophia Day
summary: Have you ever wondered how computers know to do the things they do? Programmers tell them! Every computer from calculators to drones had a programmer design how it should function. Join Aanya as she plays out the creative process of coding and learns its many fun and practical uses.

title: Here to Bump and Bump to Hear: An Assistant Dog's Journey
author: Jane M. Biehl PhD
summary: The beautiful yellow lab was abandoned and in a shelter. She did not understand why nobody wanted to adopt her. Finally, a woman stopped at her cage and took her home. She named the dog Sita and trained her, through a prison service-dog training program and her own service-dog training facility, to be a hearing ear dog. This book's colorful pages depict Sita's journey from the shelter, to a prison, and finally to a forever home where she is cherished as a companion and indispensable helper. Her true, heartwarming story is appropriate for young children. Teachers and librarians can use this book as an informational tool.

title: Hi I'm Me
author: Kelly Vurinaris
summary: In this heart-warming tale about a mother and daughter, first-time author Kelly Vurinaris captures the beauty in human differences.She takes the reader through everyday experiences in the life of Chloe, a little girl with facial differences. Since she’s been going out into the world, Chloe notices people staring at her. To connect with others and break down initial barriers, she quickly discovers that she just needs to introduce herself. With one simple phrase, she is creating inclusivity and changing the world, one face at a time. With her mom’s guidance, Chloe learns that although she may look different, all differences are beautiful.This is a book that builds confidence in our children without them understanding what confidence is. It helps kids identify that being different is okay, and that we are all people. It is the perfect story for parents or teachers who want to teach children about inclusivity, and how everyone has equal value no matter how they look.

title: Hi I'm Me in Kindergarten
author: Kelly Vurinaris
