summary: In this next book of the series, Chloe is off to Kindergarten! At first she is a little scared, but once she realizes she can be herself, she quickly discovers how much fun school can be. The lessons she learns lead to new friends and an understanding of why kindness and positivity are so important. Readers of Vurinaris' heart-warming series will see that you can build confidence by using your voice, which can take you on many adventures!

title: Hiya Moriah
author: Victoria Nelson
summary: Hiya! Meet Moriah! She is spunky and silly... and she also has CHARGE syndrome. In this book, Moriah takes you on her journey to explain medical terminology and devices in a way that's engaging and easy to understand. This is a beautiful story that encourages love and acceptance for everyone and teaches how to make a new friend by waving and saying "hiya." With its bonus picture glossary and activity page, Hiya Moriah provides education, promotes inclusion, and raises awareness about children with disabilities.

title: Holly's Hands Language Learning Activity Book
author: Eric Mosconi

title: Hope & Hearing: A Mother's Journey Through Childhood Hearing Loss
author: Cindy Dean
summary: When your child is diagnosed with hearing loss, the world can feel overwhelming. The questions come fast: Will my child talk? Make friends? Thrive in school? Be happy? You are not alone. In Hope & Hearing, Cindy Dean shares her family’s journey with her son Carson: from failing newborn hearing tests, to getting new hearing aids and FM systems, to the unexpected struggles of adolescence during COVID, and finally to the joy of watching him thrive in college. With honesty, warmth, and encouragement, Cindy offers both comfort and practical wisdom for parents just beginning this path. Through personal stories and gentle reflections, she shows that while the road is not always easy, it is filled with progress, resilience, and joy. Hope & Hearing is more than a memoir. It’s a companion for the journey, reminding parents that progress looks different for every child, but love and persistence make all the difference. Because every child deserves to be heard.

title: Horseshoe Castle: A children's guide to American Sign Language
author: April D. Pulver
summary: The second story in the series "Eight Little Hands" is titled "Horseshoe Castle", created by April Pulver. Join Elizabeth in this 14-page story as she explores the seashore, finds a unique treasure and builds a sandcastle with her papa! While reading each page, sign with Elizabeth, words used during her day at the beach. Included in this story are watercolor, hand-drawn illustrations and pictures of chosen select words, in American Sign Language (ASL). As a bonus to every story in the "Eight Little Hands" series pictures are added demonstrating the alphabet and numbers, in ASL. Also, in "Horseshoe Castle" key words used during outdoor activities are demonstrated, such as "tree", "snow", "trail", and "rock". Furthermore, "Horseshoe Castle" makes learning ASL fun, simple and enjoyable for the entire family. This allows parents to converse with their little ones long before they are able to communicate (both hearing and deaf children), thus reducing frustration and enhancing language development. Naturally, infants are inclined to use their hands and body gestures to communicate. The family learning simple signs such as "water", "cold", "big", and "dance" enhances conversation, connection, and understanding.

title: How Do You Deaf?: Finding your voice, your identity and your place in the world
author: Kerry Tidman

title: How I got My Super Ears: A Hearing Aid Story
author: Elizabeth Yeung

title: How Much Do You Know About...American Sign Language, and Deaf Culture, History, Jokes, and Stories?
author: Becky Gage
summary: Experience Deaf humor in a groundbreaking multimedia format. This book combines pop-up illustrations with QR codes linking to ASL videos, transforming classic jokes and zap stories—passed down through generations within the Deaf community—into vibrant, interactive visuals.

title: How the Deaf Boy Saved Independence Day
author: Frederick J Tirrell

title: How to Sign Series with Terry the Monkey
author: Joe Jacobs

title: Hubi Dubi: Bedside Care
author: Sebastian Metz
summary: Hubi Dubi is a young boy who goes on adventures with his best friend, Birdie. Join them in these exciting stories. Each installment of the Hubi Dubi series presents a new story for you and your child. Each page of the Hubi Dubi books features up to four American Sign Language (ASL) signs. By reading together and interacting with the signs in a fun way, your child's language skills will flourish. This read-aloud book is especially suitable for children with language acquisition impairments. However, Hubi Dubi is fun for all children. The engaging illustrations and the opportunity to imitate the signs you show will motivate and engage your child.

title: Hubi Dubi: The Busy Bee
author: Sebastian Metz
summary: Hubi Dubi is a young boy who goes on adventures with his best friend, Birdie. Join them in these exciting stories. Each installment of the Hubi Dubi series presents a new story for you and your child. Each page of the Hubi Dubi books features up to four American Sign Language (ASL) signs. By reading together and interacting with the signs in a fun way, your child's language skills will flourish. This read-aloud book is especially suitable for children with language acquisition impairments. However, Hubi Dubi is fun for all children. The engaging illustrations and the opportunity to imitate the signs you show will motivate and engage your child.

title: Hubi Dubi: The Tree House
author: Sebastian Metz
summary: Hubi Dubi is a young boy who goes on adventures with his best friend, Birdie. Join them in these exciting stories. Each installment of the Hubi Dubi series presents a new story for you and your child. Each page of the Hubi Dubi books features up to four American Sign Language (ASL) signs. By reading together and interacting with the signs in a fun way, your child's language skills will flourish. This read-aloud book is especially suitable for children with language acquisition impairments. However, Hubi Dubi is fun for all children. The engaging illustrations and the opportunity to imitate the signs you show will motivate and engage your child.

title: Hunting Sirens: A Gender-Flipped Little Mermaid
author: Mary Mecham
summary: Trapped on an island nation and surrounded by bloodthirsty sirens, the starving people of Haven Harbor are losing hope. Treva, a Deaf blacksmith immune to the tempting call of the sirens lurking in the deep, steps into the perilous role of Siren Hunter. If she can rid the waters of enough sirens, her people could be free at last. Unbeknownst to her, another creature dwells in the sea, even more ferocious than the dreaded sirens. When Treva faces the monster and loses, her life is saved by a handsome siren who has no voice. Can mortal enemies set their differences aside long enough to unite their nations? Or will Treva ignore her growing feelings and finish what she started?

title: Hydroponic Hijinks
author: Rie Neal
summary: In this third book in the Astrid the Astronaut chapter book series, Astrid is torn between her best friend and arch-rival! The Shooting Stars and Petite Picasso groups are working together on a combined STEM and art project—which means BFFs Astrid and Hallie finally get to team up! But Astrid’s excitement doesn’t last long when they are grouped with her arch-nemesis: Pearl. And what’s worse, Hallie actually agrees with some of the ideas Pearl has for the project. Isn’t she supposed to be on Astrid’s side? Can Astrid, Hallie, and Pearl learn how to work together and create a project that’s out of this world?

title: I Am Me From A to Z
author: Victoria Nelson
summary: Celebrate the value in every child and the power in every word!Join Moriah and Friends on an empowering journey through the alphabet. Through a series of positive affirmations, children will learn to recognize their own amazing qualities and appreciate the things that make everyone wonderfully unique! Following Victoria Nelson's Hiya Moriah, this book provides a bonus picture glossary of medical and adaptive devices that promotes awareness, acceptance, and inclusion for children with disabilities.

title: I Belong Here
author: Kelly Brakenhoff
summary: Meet Duke, a fun-loving second grader who loves to draw, play baseball, and explore the world around him. With his hearing aid and American Sign Language, Duke communicates in his own special way. But sometimes, fitting in can be tough. In this heartwarming chapter book, Duke navigates school, friendships, and fun, showing that everyone has their unique way to learn and play. Young readers will relate to Duke’s struggles and triumphs as he finds his place in both the hearing and deaf worlds. Perfect for ages 6-10, this story celebrates accessibility, inclusion, and the challenges of self-discovery.

title: I Can Finally Hear Birds: A Candid, Comical And Intimate Journey About Hearing Loss, Meniere's Disease And Cochlear Implants
author: Nancy J. Chovancek

title: I Can Hear You Whisper: An Intimate Journey Through the Science of Sound and Language
author: Lydia Denworth

title: I Can't Hear Like You
author: Althea
summary: I Can Wash My Hands guides children through the process of hand washing, describing when and how to wash hands to stay safe and healthy. These short stories are designed with simple, memorable scripts and illustrations focusing on a singular behavior or process.

title: I DEAF-initely Can: Rhett the Heeler
author: Karlie Waldrip
summary: "I Deaf-initely Can, Rhett the Heeler" children's book is written in Rhett's perspective as a dog who is deaf. Rhett shares his experiance about waiting for his forever home at the shelter, meeting a deaf girl (Karlie), getting adopted a few days later, learning sign language and showing the world that deaf dogs are able! Written by a deaf author and illustrated by a deaf illustrator.

title: I Felt the Cheers: The Remarkable Silent Life of Curtis Pride
author: Curtis Pride and Doug Ward

title: I Got a New Hearing Aid
author: Dr. Dana Walker

title: I Have a Hearing Loss and It's Okay
author: William M. Bauer

title: I Hear the Sunspot (3 Volume Series)
author: Yuki Fumino
summary: A heartwarming story of love and acceptance. Because of a hearing disability, university student Kohei had made a habit of distancing himself from those around him. But after meeting the exceedingly cheerful Taichi, his world begins to change, and he gradually embraces a more positive outlook on life, opening his heart. As their friendship deepens, Kohei eventually begins to see Taichi as more than a friend, and after he finally confesses his love to Taichi, the feelings become mutual. Will their love overcome the challenges they face, or will Kohei's insecurities hold them back?

title: I Know How I Feel
author: Sophia Day
summary: Join our MVP Kids in a page-flipping, mood-flopping discovery of feelings. Children will learn to name and express emotions, gaining self-awareness and coping skills as they experience the daily ups and downs of childhood.

title: I Like it Loud: A tale of Unilateral Hearing Loss
author: Dr. LaAdah Demus & Miles DeMus

title: I Talk with My Hands: The Contest
author: Jeanne Mansfield & Gail Lenhard

title: I'll Tell You Later: Deaf Survivors of Dinner Table Syndrome
author: Raymond Luczak (Editor)

title: I'm Getting a Cochlear Implant!: Learn About How I Will Hear!
author: Lindsay Dain

title: I'm Like You, Just with a Magic Ear
author: Monsther Fan

title: I'm Not Just a Deaf Kid
author: Dr. LeoNora M. Cohen

title: IEPs R4U and Me
author: Kelly Brakenhoff
summary: Meet Duke, a fun-loving second grader who loves to draw, play baseball, and explore the world around him. With his hearing aid, American Sign Language, and his trusty IEP, Duke navigates school in his own special way. Through his adventures, Duke learns that his IEP is a tool that helps him succeed and that asking for what he needs makes him strong. In this heartwarming chapter book, Duke faces the ups and downs of school, friendships, and family life. Young readers will relate to Duke’s struggles with teasing, his triumphs in learning, and his journey toward self-acceptance. Perfect for ages 6-10, this story celebrates the importance of self-advocacy, inclusion, and the joy of discovering how each of us learns best.

title: Included: A book for ALL children about inclusion, diversity, disability, equality and empathy
author: Jayneen Sanders

title: Insights Without Sound: A deaf Christian's journey to understand God and life as told through her own eyes
author: Angela Rogers

title: Invincible
author: Cecily Anne Paterson
summary: Jazmine likes Liam. At least, she thinks she does, so it's hard to understand why she feels so uncomfortable around him. On top of that, she has to deal with Gabby, her best friend who seems to have disappeared entirely, and her Grandmam who's full of challenges and family secrets.If she's going to figure it out, Jaz will need to speak up for herself, rescue her friendship, and make peace with her relationship to her changing family. But where will she get her courage from?

title: Invisible
author: Cecily Anne Paterson
summary: Being in the school drama production is the first thing that's made thirteen year old Jazmine feel alive and happy in years. But to keep her part, and her new friends, she'll have to hurdle the barrier of her deafness, stand up to the school bully and face the truth about what really happened to her dad.

title: It Will Change your Life: A Cochlear Implant Journey
author: Julieann Wallce

title: It's My Story
author: Kelly Brakenhoff
summary: Meet Duke, a fun-loving second grader who loves to draw, play baseball, and explore the world around him. With his hearing aid and American Sign Language, Duke communicates in his own unique way. Through his adventures, Duke learns that being Deaf is a part of who he is, and it doesn’t need to be fixed. In this heartwarming chapter book, Duke navigates school, friendships, and family life, showing that everyone has their unique way to learn and play. Young readers will relate to Duke’s struggles and triumphs as he embraces his identity and finds his place in both the hearing and deaf worlds. Perfect for ages 6-10, this story celebrates self-acceptance, inclusion, and the joy of discovering what makes us special.

title: Jack and the Crunchy Leaves: My First Hearing Aids
author: Kenn Heyder

title: Jack Signs!
author: Karen Hardwicke
summary: Based on a true story, Jack Signs! follows the journey of a little boy who is deaf, his typically hearing sister and their Mum. When his Mum discovers a book of sign language, their family life is transformed and Jack blossoms with his new-found communication skills. The book integrates "sign along with me" icons, which prompt children (and their parents) to sign along with Jack as they read the story. (Please note: Linked demonstration videos are currently available in British Sign Language / BSL and American Sign Language / ASL only. We hope to add further videos featuring other sign languages soon.)

title: Jack Signs Colours!
author: Karen Hardwicke
summary: Jack, Caitlin and Mummy set out to find the most colourful things in their house and garden, learning the sign for each colour as they go along. Mummy is also collecting bottles of paint, one of each colour as they get ready to create the most wonderful picture. What could possibly go wrong? Jack Signs Colours! integrates “sign along with me” icons, which prompt children (plus their parents and teachers) to sign along with Jack as they read the story. If they come across new vocabulary, they can hop online here to learn the sign from the video resources available below, and to watch the whole story signed in both BSL (British Sign Language) and ASL (American Sign Language).

title: Jack Signs Farm Animals!
author: Karen Hardwicke
summary: Today, Jack is in for a big surprise! He's not going to Nursery...he's off to THE FARM!  Accompanied by Mummy, sister Caitlin and Pink Pig, his favourite toy, Jack has fun learning the signs for all the animals as they explore the farm, then stop for a yummy picnic in the sun. (You too can learn along with Jack, by using the free ASL and BSL resources on the AVID Language website, where you can also see the whole story signed!). Then, with thirst quenched and tummies full, it's off to find more animals. Everything is going so well until... oh no! Where is Pink Pig? Jack is distraught - he wants to show his favourite toy to the real pigs, but he can't find him! What can be done to save the day? Jack Signs Farm Animals! integrates “sign along with me” icons, which prompt children (plus their parents and teachers) to sign along with Jack as they read the story. If they come across new vocabulary, they can hop online to learn the sign from the video resources available on the AVID Language website, and to watch the whole story signed in both BSL (British Sign Language) and ASL (American Sign Language).

title: Jack's New Ear Gear: A Book of Ling Six Sounds
author: Grace Andrews
summary: Jack's New Ear Gear is the story of a young boy who is just a bit afraid to use his new hearing aids for the first time. With the help of his family and community, he overcomes his fear as he discovers all the wonderful sounds there are to hear in his world. Jack's New Ear Gear features the phonemes, or sounds, that are used in the Ling Six Sound Test to assess a young child's ability to hear the low, middle, and high frequency sounds that are essential for language development. These sounds, which include [m], [ah], [oo], [ee], [sh] and [s], are paired with corresponding pictures throughout the book to encourage parents and teachers to use the Ling Six exercise in a fun and engaging way with all children.

title: Jake and Jasmine to the Rescue
author: Karen Harlow

title: Jake's the Name, Sixth Grade's the Game
author: Deb Piper
summary: Here is a humorous, first-person account by a deaf boy mainstreamed into the sixth grade of a public school. He speaks of what it is like for him and of his perceptions of others' reactions to him. Jake is a typical adolescent boy with opinions about everything, coping with the changes of growing up, trying to understand his parents who are tuned into adolescence, and trying to figure out his buddies who are now acting flat out weird around girls. He is also a scamp at heart, and his antics will make the reader laugh outloud.

title: Jasmine's Magic Hearing Machines
author: Denise Abdale
summary: Jasmine has a big dream—she wants to be a dancer! But with hearing loss, she faces challenges that others don’t always understand. After undergoing multiple surgeries and wearing special machines on her head to help her hear, Jasmine must navigate not only the world of dance but also the reactions of her classmates. In Jasmine's Magic Hearing Machines, author Denise Abdale—drawing from her experience as a teacher of the deaf, hearing-impaired, and a speech therapist—shares a heartfelt story about resilience, self-acceptance, and the power of understanding. This inspiring book helps children see the importance of inclusivity and kindness, especially for classmates who look or experience the world differently.

title: Jeet & Fudge: Dance! Dance! Dance!
author: Amandeep Kochar
summary: Get ready to move, groove, and spread kindness with Jeet, his lovable pup Fudge, and their friends in Jeet & Fudge: Dance! Dance! Dance! When the group starts rehearsing for the school talent show, everyone is excited―until their new friend Davina begins to feel discouraged. Davina is deaf, and learning the dance moves without hearing the beat proves tricky. Determined to make sure everyone can shine on stage, Jeet and his friends brainstorm creative ways to help. Along the way, Davina teaches the group some sign language, showing that friendship and teamwork can overcome any challenge. Together, they discover that the real magic of performing is making sure no one is left out.

title: Jo Bright and the Seven Bots
author: Deborah Underwood

title: Joss: Touch the Sky
author: Erin Falligant
summary: In the second book in her series: Joss Kendrick is always looking for new ways to soar. So when her cheer team needs a new flyer, she can't wait to step up (literally). Her high-flying skills on her surfboard and skateboard make her a natural for the role. But when her skateboarding act at the talent show turns into a total catastrophe -- make that a dogtastrophe -- Joss loses her confidence all around, even at the cheer gym. The cheer competition is coming up fast, though, and her team is depending on her to nail a tricky stunt. The problem is, Joss doesn't trust them not to drop her. She wants to do an easier stunt, but if she plays it safe, can her team still win? And if they don't, will her team ever forgive her?

title: Juntos Lo Podemos Lograr! Rhett el boyero australiano
author: Karlie Waldrip
summary: ¡Juntos Lo Podemos Logar! Rhett el boyero australiano" es un libro infantil escrito desde la perspectiva de Rhett, un perro sordo. Rhett comparte su experiencia sobre esperar su hogar para siempre en el refugio, conocer a una niña sorda (Karlie), ser adoptado unos días después, aprender lenguaje de señas y mostrarle al mundo que ¡los perros sordos son capaces! Escrito por un autor sordo e ilustrado por un ilustrador sordo.

title: Just an Otter Day
author: Stephanie Mae Green
summary: Just An Otter Day is a charmingly-illustrated book about a day in the life of Ollie the Otter. Ollie is an otter with a hearing loss. Join him and his family along their hearing loss journey as Ollie receives a cochlear implant and learns to listen, speak, and much more!

title: Just an Otter Day: Ollie Otter Gets a Cochlear Implant
author: Stephanie Mae Green
summary: Start at the very beginning when Ollie Otter's mother finds out Ollie has a hearing loss. Join Ollie and his mother as she decides to begin their listening and spoken language journey by getting hearing aids and a cochlear implant.

title: Kaisa and the Sounds of the Farm
author: Kidztale Club

title: Kasey's First Day of Basketball
author: Kentrell Martin, Kentrell Martin Jr.
summary: Kasey's First Day of Basketball Practice shows the importance of being yourself! Fearing what others will think about him on the first day of basketball practice, Kasey decides not to wear his hearing aid. He quickly discovers that this might not have been such a good idea after all. Kasey's First Day of Basketball Practice shows the importance of being yourself while teaching children who love basketball about the game. They learn little-known basketball facts and can access a comprehensive glossary of terms that every child who wants to play basketball should know. A full-page diagram illustrates key spots on a basketball court. In the question-and-answer section, readers can list their favorite NBA players, teams, MVP and championship prospects.

title: Kasey's Outdoor Adventure
author: Kentrell Martin, Kentrell Martin Jr.
summary: This is the very first picture book in the Shelly’s Adventures collection from Kasey! Kasey is one of Shelly’s best friends, and he loves using the sign language he’s learned from her to teach others. In every Kasey picture book, readers join Kasey as he shares new ASL signs with his friends—all while exploring the exciting places and experiences they discover together!

title: Kat's Fall
author: Shelley Hrdlitschka
summary: Darcy's mother is getting out of jail. Ten years ago she was convicted of throwing Darcy's baby sister off a fifth-floor balcony. Kat survived, but Darcy has spent the last decade raising his sister, giving her the love and support she has been denied by an absent mother and an uncaring father. Now, with their mother about to be released, their father has decided he has had enough of parental responsibility and is determined to return the children to their mother's care. Darcy is horrified, and adamant that he will not subject his sister to the woman who once tried to murder her. Struggling to understand and control the anger and resentment that has consumed him, Darcy is forced to confront his feelings and engage with the outside world. With newfound friendships and the unflagging support of his teacher, Darcy is able to understand some of the pain of the past and accept that he cannot control everything. As he grudgingly re-establishes ties with the mother he thinks he hates, he is accused of a horrific act. It will take incredible strength--his own and others--to fight the charges, but he finds that truth is often an elusive concept and that trust--and love--are powerful allies.

title: Katie Meets Carl
author: Erin Savory
summary: Katie has Down syndrome; Carl, the new boy in her class, is Deaf. Follow along as Katie uses sign language (and learns new ASL signs!) to talk to Carl and build a new friendship!

title: Kim's Quiet New Friend
author: Alessandro Reale
summary: When a new family moves in next door, Kim is excited that she will get to make a new friend. She meets Ellie, a young girl who is deaf, meaning she cannot hear very well and that she speaks using sign language. At first, Kim has trouble talking with Ellie since she doesn't know sign language. Kim decides to teach herself how to sign so that she can properly communicate with Ellie and make her new friend feel right at home.

title: Kicking Up Dirt: A True Story of Determination, Deafness, and Daring
author: Ashley Fiolek & Caroline Ryder

title: KJ's Emotional Day
author: Kentrell Martin, Kasey Martin
summary: This is the first fun-filled book in The Shelly’s Adventures collection from KJ. KJ is Shelly’s Deaf baby brother, and he communicates using American Sign Language (ASL). KJ’s series teaches signs for emotions, objects, food, numbers, places, people, pets, activities and so much more! In this book, KJ teaches the reader the signs in ASL for different emotions that he feels throughout the day.

title: Learn to Sign With Your Baby
author: Cecelia S. Grugan

title: Let's Celebrate! Favorite Foods
author: Sophia Day
summary: Discover new foods with familiar ingredients as our MVP Kids share their favorite dishes from around the world. Enjoy exploring world cuisine and be encouraged to try something new! Lift-the-flap activities reveal real photographs of delicious dishes.

title: Let's Celebrate! Getting Around
author: Sophia Day
summary: Get ready to explore the world on planes, trains, automobiles, and more with our MVP Kids! Read the story, then fold out the accordion pages for engaging tummy-time play with high-contrast illustrations that stimulate your baby’s vision. Bilingual labels help build baby’s vocabulary in Spanish and English!

title: Let's Celebrate! Music
author: Sophia Day
summary: Clap your hands and play along with our MVP Kids as they explore musical instruments from around the world! Feel the beat of each instrument with indented finger-trace paths.

title: Let's Celebrate! Playing
author: Sophia Day
summary: Ready, set, play with MVP Kids! Favorite sports, toys, and activities fill the pages of this fun, interactive story as children move from independent to cooperative play. Be part of the action by manipulating the toys with fun lift-the-flaps.

title: Let's Cook!
author: Youmee Lee
summary: Grandma is coming to visit! Grandma shows Hwa how to garden and how to make tofu from soybeans with a little bit of magic. Hwa helps Grandma in the garden, and then the whole family eats the yummy dishes that Grandma and Hwa made with love.

title: Let's Explore!
author: Youmee Lee
summary: Min and his Dad go camping and have an adventure in nature! First, they set up their tent, and then they explore together. They learn how to respect nature by not disturbing things they find. As it gets dark, they use a map to find their way back, and then they build a campfire and look at the stars. They realize that they are part of the wonderful world around them and promise to explore nature again soon.

title: Let's Go For a Sound Walk!
author: Karen Hardwicke

title: Let's Make!
author: Youmee Lee
summary: Min asks his parents whether or not he can be an artist. Mom helps him draw and mix colors to paint. Dad shows Min how to preserve memories by taking pictures with his camera. Then Min has a fun idea for the family: let's make an imaginary world! They create together, and Min learns that creating things makes him an artist. When he grows up, he can be anything he wants!

title: Let's Share!
author: Youmee Lee
summary: At playtime, Hwa really wants to play with the other children, but she also wants all the fun things for herself! This makes the other children sad because they can't play too. When a friend shares with Hwa, Hwa learns that sharing can be sweet. She starts to share too. Everyone plays together, makes friends, and has fun as they learn signs together.

title: Let's Sleep!
author: Youmee Lee
summary: At bedtime, Dad reads Min and Hwa a story about the Moon and the Sun. They wish upon a shooting star, and the stars come to their window! Min becomes the bright Sun, but Hwa is afraid of the dark. After learning about a firefly, she becomes the gentle Moon who shines at night. Just like the Korean folktale the author learned as a child, the Moon and Sun take turns lighting up the world.

title: Let's Talk About Body Boundaries: Consent and Respect
author: Jayneen Sanders

title: Lety's Deaf Diaries: I can do Anything
author: Laura Eakiri

title: Life After Deaf: My Misadventures in Hearing Loss and Recovery
author: Noel Holston

title: Lightning
author: StarAndrea
summary: The mid-term break at Smith Valley means that Kae can finally get some work done. Since Hope has managed to keep herself out of trouble for several days in a row, he accepts a reconnaissance mission to Sirian space and a followup undercover operation on Earth. The first person he encounters isn't a person at all, but the android avatar used by the Smith Valley AI: also undercover, and surprisingly willing to help him. KERI claims to be bored, and it turns out she isn't kidding. She sets herself up as his "security coordinator" and keeps an eye on anyone trying to sabotage him without being asked. She offers her opinion freely, and it's surprisingly helpful: between his family's observations and her speculation, they make Kae's company contacts work for them. While Kae and KERI pursue the Sirians' true purpose on Earth, Michiko and Jenni are on their way back to Smith Valley. The nearest transportation hub happens to be Roris' homeworld, so they get him to give them a tour during their layover. When the three of them look up Kechana to see if she'll introduce them to her dolphin friends, it turns out that she already has a visitor of her own. Hope is on Alandia, where she and Kechana are making the most of the vacation they have left.

title: Lights of Home
author: StarAndrea
summary: Jenni and Michiko graduate from Smith Valley, and they've made plans for a vacation on Calijyt before starting freelance work along the Border. Roris is headed for Eltare after a visit home, but Ziah is secretive about his intinerary. He tells Jenni only that she should stay away from Earth, and not to believe everything she sees in the news. She and Michiko arrive on Calijyt to find Hope waiting for them there. Hope is supposed to be on Alandia with Kechana, and Roris supplies proof that she is. She's also on Calijyt. Hope says she's chasing political leads in an effort to help Earth without endangering her own home, and she thinks Michiko can help. Hope's traveling companions come looking for her when she doesn't come back. Jenni bonds with Metta over language and argues with Jastavika over the same. In the meantime, Terra is on her way with a ship and news of family: their mother is heading home to Earth. When Ziah reappears with a friend they haven't seen since the mid-term break and some very important people after them, everyone's plans change.

title: Ling Ling Bird Hears with His Magic Ears
author: Tanya Saunders
summary: Ling Ling Bird Hears with his Magic Ears’ takes us on an exciting journey through the world of sound, designed to help build listening skills in young children in the early stages of learning to listen and speak. We accompany Ling Ling Bird, who is deaf and wears cochlear implants that help him to hear. Together we listen to what he hears with his super magic ears!

title: Ling Ling Bird Loves Christmas
author: Tanya Saunders
summary: As Ling Ling Bird and his sister Lola Louise find their Christmas stockings, bake a colossal Christmas cake, wait up to see Father Christmas (you might also call him Santa Claus), open presents and pull Christmas crackers while being watched over by cheeky elves on shelves, this bright and fun festive story offers an exciting and engaging way for children to learn and practice Christmas vocabulary.

title: Ling Ling Bird: Seen and Heard
author: Tanya Saunders
summary: Larger-than-life Ling Ling Bird forms an unlikely bond with tiny wee Chickadee in this uplifting tale of discovery, friendship and acceptance. While Chickadee gains a fascinating insight into the world of her dazzling new buddy who is deaf, Ling Ling Bird learns that being different does not prevent him forming special friendships. Amazing things are possible with a can-do attitude!

title: Ling Ling Bird: Who am I?
author: Tanya Saunders
summary: The ever-playful Ling Ling Bird returns with a boldly illustrated story encouraging early learners to practice new speech sounds and the ‘serve and return’ of conversation. Ling Ling Bird knows he’s a bird, but what kind of bird is he? He is determined to find out! Along the way, he not only encounters a dazzling array of other birds, but also a range of different speech sounds. He learns some new vocabulary and a critical skill for developing communication: two-way engagement. What fun it is too! And to top it all, the story ends with a joyful song, set to a familiar, family-favourite tune – when you see the words, you’ll know it straight away!

title: Listen: How Evelyn Glennie, a Deaf Girl, Changed Percussion
author: Shannon Stocker

title: Listening Closely: A Journey to Bilateral Hearing
author: Arlene Romoff

title: Listening: Ways of Hearing in a Silent World
author: Hannah Merker

title: Livyloo's Quiet Morning
author: Alexia Williams
summary: You are invited to embark on Livyloo’s journey as her pals try to wake her up in time for school.

title: Living on Chocolate
author: Dian Curtis Regan
summary: "I lived on Chocolate Circle for two months before I realized the sign on the corner referred to me: Deaf Child Area." Annie's life hasn't been easy. Losing her hearing at the age of seven was the worst thing that ever happened to her—until her mother gave her up to foster care. Now, Annie is one of many foster kids at the Moreno's. She succeeds at remaining invisible—until her first experience with "that time of the month" shines an unwanted spotlight on her. Or . . . could this be the attention she's been longing for?

title: Lizzie & Lucky: The Mystery of the Disappearing Rabbit
author: Megan Rix
summary: Join Lizzie and Lucky on another mystery-solving adventure - this time at a magic show! Young detective Lizzie and her puppy, Lucky, are off to a magic show. But during the performance, a world-famous rabbit disappears - and not on purpose! Can Lizzie and Lucky find Rudy the rabbit, get to the bottom of who stole him, and why?

title: Lizzie & Lucky: The Mystery of the Lost Chicken
author: Megan Rix
summary: Join young detective Lizzie and her adorable puppy Lucky on another mystery-solving adventure! Lizzie and Lucky are visiting the Five Freedoms Sanctuary. It's a lovely place for all sorts of rescued animals. When a local farmer announces some terrible plans for his land that could endanger the surrounding wildlife, our dynamic duo know they must investigate the case. Can they use their detective skills to stop him? While looking for clues, Lizzie spots some strange footprints and an unusual blue creature that stands out from the rest! But what is it, and where did it come from? Could it be the key to saving the day?

title: Lizzie & Lucky: The Mystery of the Missing Puppies
author: Megan Rix
summary: Lizzie is on a mission to solve the case of the disappearing puppies... Lizzie is desperate for a dog. And in order to convince her parents to get one, she has to come up with 101 reasons why she needs one. Lizzie is a master at making lists, so thinking of 101 reasons is going to be easy! Especially as she could train one to be her hearing dog. But as Lizzie begins compiling her list, she witnesses an adorable puppy being snatched away and put into a van by a mysterious-looking man. Can Lizzie solve the case - and maybe find herself a loyal friend at the same time...?

title: Lizzie & Lucky: The Mystery of the Stolen Treasure
author: Megan Rix
summary: Join Lizzie and Lucky on another mystery-solving adventure! When Lizzie and her cute puppy Lucky go on a family trip to the seaside, something doesn't seem right. There's talk of missing treasure no one has been able to find for years and years, and artefacts are mysteriously disappearing in the local museum. Anyone else would shrug it off but this dynamic duo just know there is a mystery to be solved here - and they won't leave until they figure it out!

title: Loveable Lucy, Splish Splash Backyard Bash
author: Norma Roth and Shayna Rose Penn

title: Lucia: alto y claro/ Lucy: loud and clear
author: Laila Lavan

title: Macy McMillan and the Rainbow Goddess
author: Shari Green

title: Maddie's World: The 1st Day of School
author: Maddison N. Davis

title: Maddie's World: What Students Need the Most!
author: Maddison N. Davis

title: Magnus Chase and the Gods of Asgard: The Hammer of Thor
author: Rick Riordan
series: Magnus Chase and the Gods of Asgard
summary: Thor's hammer is missing again. The thunder god has a disturbing habit of misplacing his weapon--the mightiest force in the Nine Worlds. But this time the hammer isn't just lost. It has fallen into enemy hands. If Magnus Chase and his friends can't retrieve the hammer quickly, the mortal worlds will be defenseless against an onslaught of giants. Ragnarok will begin. The Nine Worlds will burn. Unfortunately, the only person who can broker a deal for the hammer's return is the gods' worst enemy, Loki--and the price he wants is very high.

title: Magnus Chase and the Gods of Asgard: The Ship of the Dead
author: Rick Riordan
series: Magnus Chase and the Gods of Asgard
summary: Magnus Chase, a once-homeless teen, is a resident of the Hotel Valhalla and one of Odin's chosen warriors. As the son of Frey, the god of summer, fertility, and health, Magnus isn't naturally inclined to fighting. But he has strong and steadfast friends, including Hearthstone the elf, Blitzen the dwarf, and Samirah the Valkyrie, and together they have achieved brave deeds, such as defeating Fenris Wolf and battling giants for Thor's hammer, Mjolnir. Now Magnus faces his most dangerous trial yet. His cousin, Annabeth, recruits her boyfriend, Percy Jackson, to give Magnus some pointers, but will his training be enough? Loki is free from his chains. He's readying Naglfar, the Ship of the Dead, complete with a host of giants and zombies, to sail against the Asgardian gods and begin the final battle of Ragnarok. It's up to Magnus and his friends to stop him, but to do so they will have to sail across the oceans of Midgard, Jotunheim, and Niflheim in a desperate race to reach Naglfar before it's ready to sail. Along the way, they will face angry sea gods, hostile giants, and an evil fire-breathing dragon. Magnus's biggest challenge will be facing his own inner demons. Does he have what it takes to outwit the wily trickster god?

title: Magnus Chase and the Gods of Asgard: The Sword of Summer
author: Rick Riordan
series: Magnus Chase and the Gods of Asgard
summary: Magnus Chase has always been a troubled kid. Since his mother’s mysterious death, he’s lived alone on the streets of Boston, surviving by his wits, keeping one step ahead of the police and the truant officers. One day, he’s tracked down by an uncle he barely knows—a man his mother claimed was dangerous. Uncle Randolph tells him an impossible secret: Magnus is the son of a Norse god. The Viking myths are true. The gods of Asgard are preparing for war. Trolls, giants and worse monsters are stirring for doomsday. To prevent Ragnarok, Magnus must search the Nine Worlds for a weapon that has been lost for thousands of years. When an attack by fire giants forces him to choose between his own safety and the lives of hundreds of innocents, Magnus makes a fatal decision. Sometimes, the only way to start a new life is to die.

title: Major and Mynah
author: Karen Owen
summary: Join SPUD, the Super Perceptive Undercover Detectives, on their first fast-paced detective adventure. Getting your first hearing aids can be nerve-wracking; especially when you have to wear them to school. When Callie realises her new hearing aids - 'the Slugs' - give her the unique ability to communicate with Bo the Mynah bird, some of her worries are lifted. In fact, having a pair of eyes in the sky might come in handy… The duo team up with Callie's best friend, Grace, as they set out to catch a local thief who has been causing trouble all over town. Includes high-speed chases, a very peckish bird and purple POO! A new mystery series, illustrated by Louise Forshaw and perfect for reluctant readers.

title: Major and Mynah: Operation Raven
author: Karen Owen
summary: When Bo sneaks into Callie's bag on her school trip to the Tower of London, she and Grace make plans to keep him secret. But when some gold coins and jewels are stolen from the site, having a spy in the sky comes in useful! The second hilarous instalment in the Major and Mynah series.

title: Major and Mynah: Project Puffin
author: Karen Owen
summary: SPUD – the Super Perceptive Undercover Detectives – are on holiday, but find themselves in a race to solve a new mystery! When they discover puffins coated in waste oil that has been dumped in the sea, they must find out who is to blame. Can they crack the case before more damage is caused to precious sea life and the Welsh coastline?

title: Major and Mynah: Tarantula Terror
author: Karen Owen
summary: SPUD – the Super Perceptive Undercover Detectives are back and they have another mystery to solve. When Callie, Grace and Bo discover that a tarantula has escaped from the wildlife park, they must find the spider and reunite her with her spiderlings, before she dies from the cold. Can Callie’ s magical hearing aids and mynah bird Bo – the SPUD team’ s Spy in the Sky – help to find the missing creature before it’ s too late?

title: Makenna's Magic Ear
author: Ellen Waters
summary: Told through the eyes of three year old Makenna is the story of getting a cochlear implant from the discovery of the hearing loss at birth. This book was written as a tool to help parents and teachers explain the cochlear implant to the child's peers.

title: Making a Play
author: Abbi Glines
summary: Ryker Lee is finally enjoying his senior year—he has great friends, hangs out with hot girls, and is on track to get a football scholarship that will set him up for college. Despite this, a small part of him wonders if there’s more to life than parties and meaningless hookups—and if football even means as much to him as it does to his fellow teammates. And when he meets the new girl at school, his world totally changes. Aurora McClay is new to Lawton. She’s grateful that her twin brother, Hunter, is star of the football team and can help her adjust to her new school, but she’s not grateful at how overprotective he is over every person she meets. Just because she is deaf does not mean people have to treat her differently. When she meets Ryker Lee, the two of them spark an instant and intense chemistry, one that proves to be controversial not only because of Ryker’s reputation as a player, but also because of Aurora and Hunter’s father’s bigoted views about who Aurora can and can’t date. Aurora and Ryker know in their hearts that they are meant for each other. But can their relationship endure the turmoil of rumors and prejudice?

title: Making a New Friend
author: Akhand Dugar
summary: Meet Maya, a spunky girl from India who has profound hearing loss and wears cochlear implants. Meet Sid, Maya's new friend who loves learning about anything and everything! Find out how they became friends. Learn cool facts about ears, the brain, sounds, and more! Join Maya and Sid on their first adventure!

title: Making Friends!
author: Jess Stockhom
summary: Babies will love lifting the flaps in these delightful board books to find out all about themselves! Focusing on friendship, play, relaxation and cooperation, these lift-the-flap books encourage conversation.

title: Marvelous Mary: Deaf With Cochlear Implants
author: Emily Christensen

title: Mask of the Jackal
author: Christine Harris
summary: Morgan Steele is fascinated by Ancient Egypt and mummies -- until he is alone in the basement of a museum and strange things begin to happen. What starts as a school assignment ends up with Morgan involved in the disappearance of mummy, kidnapping, madness, a terrifying attempt to escape danger and a search for immortality.

title: Matilda and Her Super Ears: A Hearing Journey
author: Elizabeth Yeung

title: Matti and Massi Missions: Washington D.C.
author: Zeina Hamad
summary: Meet adventure-seeking brothers who are in a race against time to complete missions around the globe. Matti and Massi touch down in the United States of America and are handed their Mission Bag and Mission Checklist. Guided by their local Mission Coach Nia, they must perform six tasks before their flight takes off for home. Can they catch a butterfly? How many flags are there around the Washington Monument? Who is Jordan, and why is she talking with her hands? Join Matti and Massi on their journey through Washington, DC and help them collect evidence before time runs out.

title: Martell Got Game
author: Cynthia Green
summary: This heartwarming story highlights how Martell's friend, Kyle, encourages him to try out for the basketball team by reminding him that his basketball skills outshine his hearing loss challenges! Parents and children alike will enjoy the Martell's World Series, which will spark thoughtful conversations about how we are all unique but similar in many ways.

title: Martell's World You're No Different Than Me
author: Cynthia Green
summary: Martell's World, "You're No Different Than Me," tells the story of a hard-of-hearing boy that feels different because of his hearing loss. Parents and children alike will enjoy Martell's World Series, which will spark thoughtful conversations on how we are all unique but similar in many ways.

title: May I sit at Your Table
author: Grace Wolf

title: Mean Little deaf Queer: A Memoir
author: Terry Galloway

title: Meet Faith: The Story of a Deaf Dalmatian
author: Tracy Tydeman

title: Meet the Neighbors: The Cottage Adventurers
author: Ashlea FitzWeber

title: Men with Their Hands: a deaf gay novel
author: Raymond Luczak

title: Mickey on the Move: Farming
author: Michelle Wagner
summary: Mickey is on the move again! He and his mom take pride in farming and gardening together, doing everything from raising chickens to growing delicious fruits and vegetables in their backyard orchard. Mickey, who was born with deafness in both ears, has overcome obstacles to fully enjoy the great outdoors. His Aqua Cochlear implants allow him to hear and appreciate the beautiful sounds in nature without the worry of weather dampening his day!

title: Mikah's Big Move
author: Tabitha J Page
summary: Mikah monkey was born cheeky and full of energy. He was also born deaf. Even though he cannot hear, this little monkey has some special skills that help him to communicate. Can you guess what they are? When Mikah learns his family is moving, he is scared and doesn't want to go. He likes things the way they are, his routine is great! Hatching a plan, Mikah spends moving day high up in the trees. No-one can make him move. Not-one-inch! But are things as bad as Mikah thinks or does he just need a dose of bravery to make it through his latest challenge?

title: Milk and More
author: Mimi Brian Vance
summary: Baby Ellis is hungry, but he can't talk, and neither can his friends BB the Black Bear and Ducky”except, that is, to each other. What can they do? They say it with sign language. Ellis lets his hands do the talking, and soon all their bellies are full. Milk and More is the first Words by the Handful story. This series of children's concept/picture books teaches American Sign Language (ASL) to babies and their families through interactive and entertaining stories.

title: Moses Goes to a Concert
author: Isaac Millman
summary: Isaac Millman tells Moses Goes to a Concert in pictures and written English, and in American Sign Language (ASL), introducing children to the signs for some of the key words and ideas. Moses and his school friends are deaf, but like most children, they have a lot to say. They communicate in American Sigh Language, using visual signs and facial expressions. This is called signing. And even though they can't hear, they can enjoy many activities through their other senses. Today, Moses and his classmates are going to a concert. Their teacher, Mr. Samuels, has two surprises in store for them, to make this particular concert a special event.

title: Moses Goes to School
author: Isaac Millman
summary: Moses goes to a special school, a public school for the deaf. He and all of his classmates are deaf or hard-of-hearing, but that doesn't mean they don't have a lot to say to each other! They communicate in American Sign Language (ASL), using visual signs and facial expressions. Isaac Millman follows Moses through a school day, telling the story in pictures and written English, and in ASL, introducing hearing children to the signs for some of the key words and ideas. At the end is a favorite song -- "Take Me Out to the Ball Game" -- in sign!

title: Moses Goes to the Circus
author: Isaac Millman
summary: Moses and his family are going to the circus. Not just any circus but the Big Apple’s Circus of the Senses! In a single ring, there are acts by trapeze artists, acrobats, elephants, horses, and clowns – all specially designed for the deaf and hard-of-hearing and the blind. Moses’s little sister, Renee, isn’t deaf but is learning sign language, and Moses loves teaching her the signs for their day at the circus. Isaac Millman takes readers on a wonderful outing in pictures and written English and in American Sign Language (ASL). Detailed diagrams of the signs are included so that readers can learn along with Renee.

title: Moses Sees a Play
author: Isaac Millman
summary: Actors from the Little Theatre of the Deaf are coming to Moses' school, and Moses and his classmates are going to see a play! A class from another school joins them, and Moses is introduced to Manuel, who has just moved to the United States. Manuel doesn't know English or sign language yet. Moses, being deaf, knows how hard it can be when no one understands you, so he tries communicating with Manuel using body gestures, while also teaching him some simple signs. This delightful book about Moses incorporates clear and colorful pictures, written English, and American Sign Language (ASL). Detailed diagrams of the signs are included so that readers can learn along with Manuel.

title: Moth Metamorphosis: A Bug Club Story
author: Gina Gallois
summary: The Bug Club is back and they're staying up late to look for moths. Things don’t go exactly as planned, until they stumble upon a promethea moth laying her eggs on a tulip tree leaf. Jaylen, Hyacinth, and Sakura have a lot to learn about these mysterious nocturnal insects and their amazing metamorphosis. What fascinating facts will they discover about giant silk moths? This book is 36 pages of inclusive, creative, educational fun for kids 3 to 8.

title: Mr. Quigley's Keys
author: Barbara Gruener
summary: Adapted from and inspired by the real-life story of an unsung hero, Mr. Quigley's Keys invites you to walk in the work shoes of a beloved handyman as he quietly jingles through the hallways of his school, listening for ways to serve and connecting by heart. Bask in the admiration and pride that the students feel for their Navy veteran, whose war injury left him deaf, and watch as his every move models the school's keys to connection: love, perseverance, work ethic, empathy, goodness, and peace. Spend a birthday in the cafeteria and experience the joy of receiving a Quigley creation, then savor the sweetness as the can-doer classmates turn the tables to thank their faithful fix-it friend. Flip over the final page for a key twist that'll wrap you up in a huge hug of gratitude and love.

title: My ABC Signs of Animal Friends
author: Ben Bahan

title: My Brother Doesn’t Speak, But His Heart Does!
author: Gabby Cornelio

title: My Cochlear Implants; Hearing is Beautiful
author: Sahar Reiazi

title: My Dawg Koa
author: Kelly Brakenhoff
summary: Deaf friends Duke and Koa are the same, but they are also different. Duke the Deaf Dog learns about similarities and differences between friends and families. A fun, engaging way to teach children that best friends share common interests. Their houses may look different, and their parents may have different rules and expectations, but Duke finds one thing families share no matter what: LOVE.

title: My Deaf Friend Can Do Anything You Can Do
author: Tanesha Ausby

title: My Family Loves Me
author: Sophia Day
summary: Families have many different ways of showing love. Follow our MVP Kids throughout their day as simple actions display loving support. Explore a wide range of family relationships and discover how to say "I love you" in twelve MVP Kids heritage languages.

title: My Hard of Hearing Life: Stories From Behind the Hearing Aids
author: Cynthia Dixon

title: My Haunting Love
author: J.S. Wilsoncroft
summary: Calista Rose is a 16-year-old deaf girl who senses something strange in her new home. Soon she meets a ghost named John and becomes smitten with his handsome good looks and charming ways. Little does she know, falling in love with him brings out the true evil that lingers inside her home. Can she survive My Haunting Love?

title: My Hearing Aids are Lost!
author: Paula Villaboim Farrugia
summary: Join the adventure as a playful rhyme leads the way through a busy house in the quest for lost hearing aids. From the bedroom to the kitchen, then the bathroom to the backyard, the search is on. This book speaks directly to every child with hearing loss who has ever felt the panic of misplacing their hearing aids, offering a reassuring reminder that they are not alone. Where could the lost hearing aids be?

title: My Heart Glows: Alice Cogswell, Thomas Gallaudet, and the Birth of American Sign Language
author: Emily Arnold McCully

title: My Island: The True Story of a Silent Challenge
author: Maggie Gordon & Hamish Rosie

title: My Journey Through Four Worlds: Growing Up in the Japanese, Deaf, Hearing, and American Worlds
author: Ronald M Hirano

title: My Monster Truck Goes Everywhere with Me
author: Kathleen Marcath

title: My Noisy Christmas: A Ling Book
author: Charis Selfridge-Poor
summary: In this follow up to My Noisy House- A Ling story, we return to the loveable, loud family on Christmas Eve. When something shakes the young boy awake,what could it be? As he listens, he hears the owl hoot 'Oo' Baby Mays loud 'Wah'. The pet snakes 'Ss' and the mouse's 'Ee' or is it something else that has woken him on this Christmas Eve night? Join the family on the festive rhyming tale, where every ling sounds brings the story to life.

title: My Noisy House: A Ling Story
author: Charis Selfridge-Poor

title: My Noisy Trip: A Ling Story
author: Charis Selfridge-Poor
summary: My Noisy Trip is a beautifully illustrated rhyming bedtime story about a family that travels, full of fun adventures and familiar animals sounds. This loving family story also helps hearing impaired children and kids with cochlear implants and hearing aids learn all six necessary Ling sounds for proper hearing and pronunciation of words.

title: My Quiet Life in 8 Counts: A Story About a Girl Who Dances to Her Own Beat
author: Kendall Stilwell
summary: A true story of dancing beyond sound. Kendall was born with bilateral sensorineural hearing loss. But in her world, rhythm is something you feel with your feet, your heart, and your whole being. From her first plié in pink ballet shoes to a spotlight performance that silenced the room, Kendall learns to move to a beat that isn't always heard—but always felt. Told in eight lyrical chapters, this illustrated novel follows Kendall through dance classes, competitions, and quiet moments of doubt and joy. Along the way, she navigates new teachers, cochlear implants, improvised routines, and big dreams—all while discovering that dancing off beat can sometimes be the most powerful performance of all.

title: My Senses
author: Sophia Day
summary: Join our MVP Kids as they discover how our senses work together to help us explore. Out on the trail is the perfect place to be, where we can touch sticky mud, rough sticks, soft grass, and other things that can’t break! Our MVP Kids also see colorful flowers, hear birds and streams, smell the woods, taste delicious hiking snacks, and much more! With delightful illustrations and pleasant rhyming text, this sturdy book is sure to charm your senses.

title: My Thingamajigs
author: L.C. Dean
summary: This book tells the story of a young boy exploring a farm and enjoying the sounds of the animals, thanks to his hearing aids, which he affectionately calls his "thingamajigs." Through stories like these, we hope to raise awareness about the use and benefits of hearing aids, while also providing representation for children who are hard of hearing.

title: Need a Friend? Learning to Sign with Rennon
author: Nicole Latimer

title: Neither-Nor: A Young Australian's Experience with Deafness
author: Paul Jacobs

title: Never Ever Build a Blanket Fort
author: Travis D Peterson
summary: Never Ever Build a Blanket Fort!—a book about bravery for kids featuring two siblings whose simple blanket fort transforms into something extraordinary through the power of imagination. Biblical values inspired by the Full Armor of God in Ephesians 6.

title: Never Mind
author: Kelly Brakenhoff
summary: “Never mind” is not allowed in Duke’s house—and for good reason. Whether it happens at preschool or the park, feeling left out hurts. Never Mind helps kids recognize that everyone deserves to be part of the conversation, especially Deaf and hard of hearing children.

title: New Beginnings, Acquiring and Living with a Cochlear Implant
author: Michael Stinson & Gerry Buckley

title: Noah Learns A Lot
author: Tramaine Robinson
summary: The doctor said the only way he'd learn to communicate is through ASL. This book teaches the days of the week, American Sign Language vocabulary and shares the victory of a boy who thrives by the mere fact of being surrounded with love; it's those around him who also learn that love is displayed. They learn to speak they way he speaks, it doesn't have to be with words. If you are looking for "if-then" examples to see resilience by structuring day to day routines, this book is just for you and your love ones. Share in this journey Noah Learns A Lot!

title: No Excuses: Growing Up Deaf and Achieving My Super Bowl Dreams
author: Derrick Coleman Jr. and Marcus Brotherton

title: No Stars at the Circus
author: Mary Finn
summary: A cross between The Boy in Striped Pyjamas and The Diary of Anne Frank. No Stars at the Circus is the beautifully told story of 10-year-old Jonas Albers, as written in his notebooks. Jonas lives in hiding in the Professor's house during the six months following the round-up of Jews in Paris on 16 July 1942. Jonas spends his days reading about his favourite subjects – among which are sharks, salmon and albatrosses. He also writes about his present life in the attic, as well as the past, in which the circumstances of his rescue are revealed. He writes about his friends at the circus and the family he greatly misses. Unaware of the atrocities happening around him and throughout Europe, Jonas hears that his parents have gone off "to work" and is worried about his little sister, Nadia, who is deaf – so worried that one day he steps outside in the hope of finding out where she is.

title: Normal: A Mother and Her Beautiful Son
author: Magdalena Newman

title: Northlander
author: Meg Burden
summary: The debut novel of a young American writer creates a world of fantasy, where the land is divided into two-- the Northlands and the Southlands.Ellin, a girl from the Southland, is forced to go with her physician father to heal the Northland king, even though Southlanders are despised and feared throughout the cold country. Ellin must find a way to battle both the people from the North and then her own people, the Southlanders, to survive in a icy and hostile land.

title: Not Fade Away: A Memoir of Senses of Lost and Found
author: Rebecca A. Alexander & Sascha Alper

title: Now Hear This: Harper Soars With Her Magic Ears
author: Valli Gideons

title: Oaklia Implant
author: Dr. Zeenat Abdulshakur
summary: Ray has Auditory Neuropathy Spectrum Disorder (ANSD). A complex hearing disorder whereby sometimes she has difficulty understanding speech despite being on hearing aids for the past 6 years. Recently, the audiologist and speech therapist advised cochlear implantation for Ray, to improve her hearing and speech. From frequently asking “what did you say?” Ray started questioning “this has sound?”.In simple language with colourful illustrations, this book aids children to understand what cochlear implants are all about.

title: Oh no! Granny Lost Her Hearing Aid in Africa!
author: Mindy Sawyer
series: Oh no! Granny Lost Her Hearing Aid!

title: Oh no! Granny Lost her Hearing Aid in Australia!
author: Mindy Sawyer
series: Oh no! Granny Lost Her Hearing Aid!

title: Oh no! Granny Lost her Hearing Aid in the Amazon!
author: Mindy Sawyer
series: Oh no! Granny Lost Her Hearing Aid!

title: Olivia Uproots the Arrogant Weed
author: Sophia Day
summary: Olivia is talented, and she knows it. But her confidence has overgrown into arrogance, choking out compassion and respect. Can a humble garden gnome help her cut back judgment and clear prejudice? Dig deep with Olivia to cultivate a self-esteem that balances pride of accomplishment with humility.

title: On The Fence: The Hidden World of the Hard of the Hearing
author: Mark Drolsbaugh

title: Orchid of the Bayou: A Deaf Woman Faces Blindness
author: Cathryn Carroll and Catherine Hoffpauir Fischer

title: Our Community
author: Sophia Day
summary: Join our MVP Kids in imagining the community roles and careers they might fulfill someday. Doctors, mechanics, emergency services, mail carriers, and so many others keep our communities running well. You can make a difference in your community today! Find out about helpful roles kids can fulfill now, and be inspired to show friends and neighbors how much you care.

title: Our Differences
author: Sophia Day
summary: Learn to be confident in who you are while respecting the uniqueness of others. By interacting with children of various differences in background, ethnicity, ability, and needs, our MVP Kids learn that differences aren’t scary and that finding common ground helps build strong friendships in which differences can be celebrated.

title: Out on the Trail
author: Sophia Day
summary: Come along and explore your five senses on the nature trail. Join our MVP Kids as they play with sticky mud, rough sticks, and soft grass, and enjoy all the sensory experiences nature has to offer. Find colorful flowers, hear birds and streams, smell the woods, taste delicious hiking snacks, and more!

title: Pantomime
author: Christopher Sebela, David Stoll, Dearbhla Kelly, Justin Birch
summary: Haley and her brother, Max, are alone after their mother’s death and are sent to Wayfair Academy, a special needs boarding school. Eventually, they find family amongst other deaf children. One night the group decides to dip their toes into crime… the thrill is too much to leave behind. They soon find out that stealing from the wrong person, has its consequences. With no one to turn to but each other, they must make a choice, one where no one comes out the same.

title: Papa, Can You Hear Me Now?
author: Dr. Amit Gosalia
summary: Hearing loss affects many aspects of our lives and Anna experiences what happens when her grandfather, Papa, struggles to communicate with her. After seeing an audiologist, Papa's relationship with his granddaughter changes! Join Anna and her family as they learn the importance of better hearing!

title: Pat-A-Cake: First Book of Nursery Rhymes
author: Ailie Busby

title: Phase Out Forgetfullness
author: Sophia Day
summary: Sometimes it’s hard to remember manners, bringing things to school, or doing chores. What creative ways can Annie, Aanya, and Lucas come up with to help them Phase Out Forgetfulness? Join our MVP Kids by reading these short stories on becoming responsible.

title: Pick Your Promises
author: Sophia Day
summary: Can our MVP Kids learn the value of keeping their promises? Join Aanya, Yong, and Frankie as they learn the importance of keeping their word to their family and friends. Pick Your Promises with our MVP Kids as you read these short stories on becoming dependable.

title: Piglet Comes Home: How a Deaf Blind Pink Puppy Found His Family
author: Melissa Shapiro, DVM

title: Pink Princess: Grandparents' House
author: Karla Fernandes
summary: Pink Princess is visiting her grandparents. She loves being with them and creating sweet memories. She is hard of hearing princess that does not allow it to stop her from learning about anything.This book is perfect for your little one that is learning to read. This is a short story with simple sentences, where your little one will learn new words and practice reading. The books in the Pink Princess collection are inspired by real girls. In each book we will portrait a different girl to celebrate the diversity that exists in children.

title: Playing Through the Seasons: Budding Flowers, Breezy Days
author: Essie Bell
summary: Playing through the Seasons: Budding Flowers, Breezy Days is all about springtime fun. As flowers bloom and breezes blow, a dad and his daughter head outside with their slow but steady pet turtle to soak in the joys of spring. From kite flying to puddle jumping, this rhyming story celebrates the little wonders of the world waking up again. Whether it is playing in the rain or racing boats in a river, this charming story highlights the beauty of inclusion, imagination, and family traditions wrapped up in the rainiest season of all.

title: Plugged In: Dancing Under the Punch Bowl
author: Peter McEachen (Oticon)

title: Plugged In: The Everyday Adventures of Thomas and his BFF, Vinton
author: Peter McEachen (Oticon)
summary: Thomas, the hero of the new Plugged In comic strip, is a typical fun-loving boy who navigates the ups and downs of middle school with a sense of adventure – and a hearing loss. Oticon Pediatrics is bringing Thomas and his humorous antics to children with hearing loss through an exclusive arrangement with Plugged In’s creator, cartoonist and toy designer Pete McEachen. “Thomas is a regular guy who experiences many funny, embarrassing and heart-warming adventures that kids of all ages will identify with,” says Maureen Doty, AuD, Product and Marketing Manager of Oticon Pediatrics. “For young people with hearing loss, Thomas has an added appeal. He allows them to laugh with him at the little mis-steps or awkward moments that are bound to occur in an active, busy life when you have a hearing loss.”

title: Police Protect and Serve
author: Sophia Day
summary: His feet may be small, but Frankie is already following in his dad’s footsteps. Meet Sergeant Frank, the friendliest policeman on Main Street! With a belt full of tools and a badge on his chest, he works tirelessly to protect and serve his community. Aspiring police will love learning the many roles of the occupation through the imagination of this playful apprentice!

title: Portal Through the Pond
author: David K. Anderson
summary: When her grandmother dies, 13-year-old Christy inherits an old family secret: the pond behind her house is in fact a portal to another world. What’s more, she learns that her grandfather went through the portal when he mysteriously “disappeared” nine years ago. Christy tries to respect her grandmother’s final wishes and not go exploring, but when a classmate named Rob falls into the pond, she has to act. Since no one would believe her if she told them the truth about the pond, Christy arranges her own rescue party. In order to rescue Rob, she’ll have to brave a bizarre alien landscape, evade hostile creatures, and protect Danny, the boy from next door who followed her through the portal. Meanwhile on Earth, the grown-ups launch a frantic search, and they’re willing to drain the pond to find out what happened. Will Christy be able to find her grandfather, rescue Rob, and return safely to Earth before she becomes a permanent resident of the Empty World?

title: Preemie Powered: The Story of SoundWave
author: Maria Addison Gray

title: Princess Lizzie Learns Manners
author: Tosombra Kimes
summary: Princess Lizzie Learns Manners is the first book of the Princess Lizzie Series. The main character, Lizzie, is modeled after an energetic and artsy little girl born with bilateral hearing loss. Her adventures in learning, exploring, and overcoming are expressed in a fun and humorous way. In Princess Lizzie Learns Manners, Lizzie discovers that manners are more than just saying please and thank you. Good manners involve helping, listening, and thinking of others. Children, parents, and educators alike can enjoy the insightful experience that each Princess Lizzie book brings. The Princess Lizzie series is geared towards early readers from preschool through 2nd grade.

title: Princess Lizzie Wears Hearing Aids
author: Tosombra Kimes
summary: What is hearing loss? Why do Princess Lizzie and other kids have to wear hearing aids? How do they help? In this book, kids learn about hearing loss by following Princess Lizzie's journey. This book is excellent for all readers, those with and without hearing loss, to enjoy.

title: Probably the World’s Best Story about a Dog and the Girl Who Loved Me
author: D. James Smith

title: Prudence Parker and a Sign of Friendship
author: Christine Burk

title: Rally Caps
author: Stephen J. Cutler and Jodi Michelle Cutler

title: Reading Between the Lips: A Totally Deaf Man Makes It in the Mainstream
author: Lew Golan

title: Ready to Be Heard: How I Lost My Hearing and Found My Voice
author: Amanda McDonough

title: Rebuilt: My Journey Back to the Hearing World
author: Michael Chorost

title: Resilient Silence: A Deaf-Experience Suspense Novel
author: Peter M. Quint

title: Resistance
author: Janet Graber
summary: In German-occupied Normandy, France, fifteen-year-old Marianne worries that her mother is exposing the family, especially Marianne's deaf younger brother, to great danger by volunteering for more perilous assignments in the resistance movement.

title: Ricky the Rock That Couldn't Roll
author: Jay Miletsky
summary: From zippy little pebbles to big strong boulders, the rock friends get together to play and roll around their favorite hill, only to find that one of their friends, Ricky, can't roll with them. Unlike all of the other rocks, who are all round, Ricky can't roll because he's flat on one side. Determined not to leave their friend behind, the rocks set out to help Ricky roll - one way or another.

title: Ricky the Rock That Just Couldn't Rhyme
author: Jay Miletsky

title: Riley The Rainbow Unicorn Meets A New Friend
author: Andrew O'Brien

title: Riley The Rainbow Unicorn And Their Magical Hearing Adventure
author: Andrew O'Brien

title: Rise and Shine: A children's guide to American Sign Language
author: April D. Pulver
summary: The first story in the series "Eight Little Hands" is titled "Rise and Shine", created by April Pulver. Join Abrahm in this 14-page story as he zips about the house getting ready for school. While reading each page, sign with Abrahm common words and phrases used in your morning routine! Included in this story are watercolor hand-drawn illustrations and pictures of chosen select words, in American Sign Language (ASL). As a bonus to every story in the series, including "Rise and Shine" are demonstrations of the alphabet, numbers, colors, and foods signed in ASL, making this book a total of 34 pages. Furthermore, "Rise and Shine" makes learning ASL fun, simple and enjoyable for the entire family. This allows parents to converse with their little one long before children are able to communicate (both hearing and deaf children), thus reducing frustration and enhancing language development. Naturally, infants are inclined to use their hands and body gestures to communicate. The family learning simple signs such as, "ready", "good morning", and "finished/all done" enhances conversation, connection, and understanding.

title: River of Hands: Deaf Heritage Stories
author: Various Authors

title: Roar of Silence: Trial & Triumph Through Deafness
author: Bob Schaller

title: Robot Rebellion
author: Rie Niel
summary: This is the fourth book in the Astrid the Astronaut chapter book series about a young girl determined to be the first hard-of-hearing astronaut in space! When Astrid and the rest of the Shooting Stars find out that their teacher, Ms. Ruiz, is about to celebrate a big birthday, they band together to create the perfect gift. Her big day happens to be the same as the upcoming robotics competition and they decide to do something there. With the help of Astrid’s big sister, Stella, they program their robot to do a fun dance and give Ms. Ruiz a sweet surprise. But on the day of the competition, the data for the Shooting Stars robot somehow gets mixed up with another one! Will their fun present end up being a major malfunction?

title: Rolling Warrior: The incredible, sometimes awkward, true story of a rebel girl on wheels who helped spark a revolution
author: Judith Heumann, Kristen Joiner

title: Ruby Rose and Sophia's Way of Hearing
author: Martha Medina
summary: When a new student named Sophia joins the class, her classmates are curious. She’s Deaf and Hard of Hearing—but what does that really mean? With the help of Ruby Rose and a classroom activity, the students learn that hearing the world differently doesn’t mean you’re broken. It just means you experience sound in your own unique way. Through gentle storytelling and vibrant illustrations, this book teaches children the power of kindness, empathy, and listening with more than just our ears. Whether through hearing aids, sign language, or open hearts, Ruby Rose and Sophia’s Way of Hearing helps every child feel seen, included, and celebrated.

title: Runaway
author: StarAndrea
summary: Smith Valley's mid-term break is a welcome vacation for its students. Jenni and Michiko made plans together, and their first stop is the frontier planet Elisia: Jenni's homeworld, where her parents and twin sister will be waiting. The last day of school is relaxed, the shuttle schedule is tight, and the nearest transportation hub is packed with departing students. Michiko and Jenni run into friends at the local space station, and they pass the time waiting for their next flight by buying souvenirs and testing out visual translators. Though some of Jenni's friends have learned to sign, others continue to rely on her transcriber to be understood. Ziah thinks it's only fair that he get a device that lets him understand her in return. Local hubs and public shuttles like a luxury to Hope when her family shows up at school to collect her and Kae. She's hustled home to the colony moon of RS-42 and wants to leave as soon as she arrives. She calls up some friends instead, joins an artistic roleplay and a telepathic retreat, and avoids her brother as much as possible. Old friends and new welcome her, but away from Smith Valley, it's impossible to know whether they like her for who she is... or for who she knows.

title: Rune: Tale of the Obsidian Maze
author: Carlos Sanchez
summary: After defeating the Shadow King, Chiri and Dai are settling into their new lives in the magical land of Puddin’. However, there is always more evil to fight against, and this time, the evil is coming to them. After being invited to the Obsidian Maze to fight the sinister MidKnight, Chiri and Dai are about to encounter the deepest darkness there ever was, and they’re not sure if they’re both going to make it safely back to their friends.

title: Rune: The Tale of a Thousand Faces
author: Carlos Sanchez
summary: Chiri and best friend Dai live in an orphanage right next to the deepest, darkest forest. On a scavenging mission to find some amazing new ingredients for her many culinary experiments, Chiri plunges both her and her best friend into the secret kingdom of Puddin’, a secret place plagued by the Thousand Faces Monster and inhabited by all sorts of magical people and critters. Befriending witches, bards, ogres and sorcerers, can our two protagonists make it home and more importantly, can they evade the darkness that has awoken on their arrival?

title: Rune: The Tale of the Winged Sorceress
author: Carlos Sanchez
summary: With Dai missing, Chiri is desperately rallying her fellow adventurers on a quest to find him. But with the darkness creating a fearsome army of creatures and planning on total domination of the kingdom, finding her best friend is starting to feel impossible. With another dark herald hunting Chiri down, it’s going to take every drop of magic she can summon to protect the friends she has left – and find the one she’s lost.

title: Sadiq and Hooyo's Drum
author: Siman Nuurali
summary: Sadiq is looking forward to being a drummer in the school band when he's old enough. When Hooyo shows Sadiq a family heirloom--a drum called a durbaan--Sadiq can't wait to show his mother's drum to his friends at school. His new friend Gabi, who's deaf, shares his love of drums. But then a mishap occurs with Hooyo's durbaan. How will Sadiq explain it to Hooyo, and can he make things right?

title: Sammy and His Super Ears
author: Kay Hoffman Brocato
summary: Sammy Sea turtle wears cochlear implants. The other animals in the ocean think he is funny looking and different. But he shows them that he really isn't different, he is just a sea turtle with SUPER EARS that likes to play just like they do.

title: Sammy and the Great Lakes Adventure
author: Frederick Miller III
summary: Dive into an enchanting journey with “Sammy’s Great Lakes Adventure”! This captivating hardcover children’s book is a treasure trove of exploration and discovery, perfect for young adventurers aged 4 to 8. Inside, young readers will embark on a thrilling voyage across the Great Lakes, accompanied by Sammy, a lovable character who encounters fascinating boats and learns about American Sign Language along the way.
